 1: ---
 2: name: web-design-guidelines
 3: description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
 4: metadata:
 5:   author: vercel
 6:   version: "1.0.0"
 7:   argument-hint: <file-or-pattern>
 8: ---
 9: 
10: # Web Interface Guidelines
11: 
12: Review files for compliance with Web Interface Guidelines.
13: 
14: ## How It Works
15: 
16: 1. Fetch the latest guidelines from the source URL below
17: 2. Read the specified files (or prompt user for files/pattern)
18: 3. Check against all rules in the fetched guidelines
19: 4. Output findings in the terse `file:line` format
20: 
21: ## Guidelines Source
22: 
23: Fetch fresh guidelines before each review:
24: 
25: ```
26: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md
27: ```
28: 
29: Use WebFetch to retrieve the latest rules. The fetched content contains all the rules and output format instructions.
30: 
31: ## Usage
32: 
33: When a user provides a file or pattern argument:
34: 1. Fetch guidelines from the source URL above
35: 2. Read the specified files
36: 3. Apply all rules from the fetched guidelines
37: 4. Output findings using the format specified in the guidelines
38: 
39: If no files specified, ask the user which files to review.

================
File: .github/copilot-instructions.md
================
   1: # Professional Development Guidelines
   2: 
   3: > **Purpose**: Enterprise-grade standards for scalable, secure, maintainable Laravel + Inertia.js application. Follow DRY principles, enforce separation of concerns, and prioritize code quality.
   4: 
   5: ## Stack
   6: - **Backend**: Laravel 12 with Inertia.js adapter
   7: - **Frontend**: React 18 + Inertia.js (SSR, no API layer)
   8: - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 + HeadlessUI
   9: - **Auth**: Laravel Breeze (session-based)
  10: - **Tools**: Ziggy for route helpers, Laravel Pint for code style
  11: 
  12: ## Build and Test
  13: ```bash
  14: # Initial setup
  15: composer run setup
  16: 
  17: # Development (runs server, queue, logs, vite concurrently)
  18: composer run dev
  19: 
  20: # Run tests (REQUIRED before commits)
  21: composer run test
  22: 
  23: # Code formatting (auto-fix)
  24: ./vendor/bin/pint
  25: ```
  26: 
  27: ---
  28: 
  29: ## Naming Conventions
  30: 
  31: ### Files & Directories
  32: ```
  33: ✓ Controllers:     UserController.php, DataRequestController.php
  34: ✓ Services:        UserService.php, DataRequestService.php
  35: ✓ Actions:         CreateUser.php, ProcessDataRequest.php (or CreateUserAction.php)
  36: ✓ Repositories:    UserRepository.php, DataRequestRepository.php
  37: ✓ Policies:        UserPolicy.php, DataRequestPolicy.php
  38: ✓ Requests:        StoreUserRequest.php, UpdateProfileRequest.php
  39: ✓ Events:          UserRegistered.php, DataRequestSubmitted.php
  40: ✓ Listeners:       SendWelcomeEmail.php, NotifyAdminOfRequest.php
  41: ✓ Jobs:            ProcessDataExport.php, SendNotificationEmail.php
  42: ✓ Middleware:      EnsureUserIsAdmin.php, LogRequestData.php
  43: ✓ Components:      PrimaryButton.jsx, DataTable.jsx, UserAvatar.jsx (PascalCase)
  44: ✓ Pages:           Index.jsx, Create.jsx, Edit.jsx, Show.jsx
  45: ✓ Hooks:           useAuth.js, useDebounce.js, useLocalStorage.js
  46: ✓ Utils:           formatDate.js, validateEmail.js, parseApiError.js
  47: ```
  48: 
  49: ### Database
  50: ```
  51: ✓ Tables:          users, data_requests, request_types (plural, snake_case)
  52: ✓ Pivot tables:    request_user, data_type_request (alphabetical order)
  53: ✓ Foreign keys:    user_id, data_request_id, parent_id
  54: ✓ Columns:         created_at, updated_at, email_verified_at (snake_case)
  55: ✓ Indexes:         idx_users_email, idx_requests_status
  56: ```
  57: 
  58: ### Code Elements
  59: ```php
  60: // Classes: PascalCase
  61: class UserService {}
  62: class CreateUserAction {}
  63: 
  64: // Methods: camelCase, descriptive
  65: public function createUser(array $data): User {}
  66: public function findActiveUsers(): Collection {}
  67: public function updateUserProfile(User $user, array $data): bool {}
  68: 
  69: // Boolean methods: is/has/can prefix
  70: public function isActive(): bool {}
  71: public function hasPermission(string $permission): bool {}
  72: public function canEditRequest(DataRequest $request): bool {}
  73: 
  74: // Variables: camelCase, descriptive (NOT $id, use $userId)
  75: $userId = 1;
  76: $isVerified = true;
  77: $dataRequest = DataRequest::find($id);
  78: $activeUsers = User::where('is_active', true)->get();
  79: 
  80: // Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
  81: const MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE = 10485760;
  82: const REQUEST_STATUS_PENDING = 'pending';
  83: ```
  84: 
  85: ### Routes
  86: ```php
  87: // RESTful: kebab-case URLs
  88: Route::get('/data-requests', [DataRequestController::class, 'index'])->name('data-requests.index');
  89: Route::post('/data-requests', [DataRequestController::class, 'store'])->name('data-requests.store');
  90: Route::get('/user-profile', [ProfileController::class, 'show'])->name('profile.show');
  91: 
  92: // ✗ Avoid: /dataRequests, /userProfile (camelCase URLs)
  93: ```
  94: 
  95: ### Tests
  96: ```php
  97: // Mirror source structure, descriptive names
  98: test_user_can_create_data_request()
  99: test_admin_can_approve_pending_requests()
 100: test_validation_fails_with_invalid_email()
 101: test_unauthorized_user_cannot_delete_request()
 102: ```
 103: 
 104: ---
 105: 
 106: ## Architectural Standards
 107: 
 108: ### File Structure for New Patterns
 109: ```
 110: app/
 111: ├── Actions/              # Single-responsibility operations
 112: │   ├── CreateUser.php
 113: │   ├── ProcessDataRequest.php
 114: │   └── SendNotificationEmail.php
 115: ├── Services/             # Business logic orchestration
 116: │   ├── UserService.php
 117: │   ├── DataRequestService.php
 118: │   └── NotificationService.php
 119: ├── Repositories/         # Data access abstraction
 120: │   ├── UserRepository.php
 121: │   ├── DataRequestRepository.php
 122: │   └── Contracts/
 123: │       ├── UserRepositoryInterface.php
 124: │       └── DataRequestRepositoryInterface.php
 125: ├── Policies/             # Authorization logic
 126: │   ├── UserPolicy.php
 127: │   └── DataRequestPolicy.php
 128: ├── Events/               # Domain events
 129: │   ├── UserRegistered.php
 130: │   └── DataRequestSubmitted.php
 131: ├── Listeners/            # Event handlers
 132: │   ├── SendWelcomeEmail.php
 133: │   └── NotifyAdminOfRequest.php
 134: └── Exceptions/           # Custom exceptions
 135:     ├── DataRequestNotFoundException.php
 136:     └── InsufficientPermissionsException.php
 137: 
 138: resources/js/
 139: ├── Hooks/               # Custom React hooks
 140: │   ├── useAuth.js
 141: │   ├── useDebounce.js
 142: │   └── useLocalStorage.js
 143: ├── Utils/               # Helper functions
 144: │   ├── formatters.js    # formatDate, formatCurrency, formatFileSize
 145: │   ├── validators.js    # validateEmail, validatePhone, validateFileType
 146: │   ├── helpers.js       # cn (classnames), truncate, chunk, sleep
 147: │   └── api.js           # API utilities (if needed)
 148: ├── Constants/           # App constants & enums
 149: │   ├── index.js         # Re-export all constants
 150: │   ├── statuses.js      # REQUEST_STATUS, labels, colors
 151: │   ├── config.js        # DATA_TYPES, MAX_FILE_SIZE, etc.
 152: │   └── routes.js        # Route constants (if needed)
 153: └── Types/               # TypeScript definitions (future)
 154:     └── index.d.ts
 155: ```
 156: 
 157: ### Controllers: Thin, Delegate Only
 158: ```php
 159: // ✓ GOOD: Thin controller, delegates to service
 160: class DataRequestController extends Controller
 161: {
 162:     public function __construct(
 163:         private DataRequestService $service
 164:     ) {}
 165: 
 166:     public function store(StoreDataRequestRequest $request): RedirectResponse
 167:     {
 168:         $dataRequest = $this->service->createRequest(
 169:             $request->user(),
 170:             $request->validated()
 171:         );
 172: 
 173:         return redirect()
 174:             ->route('data-requests.show', $dataRequest)
 175:             ->with('success', 'Data request created successfully.');
 176:     }
 177: }
 178: 
 179: // ✗ BAD: Business logic in controller
 180: public function store(Request $request)
 181: {
 182:     $validated = $request->validate([...]); // Should be FormRequest
 183:     
 184:     DB::transaction(function () use ($validated) { // Should be in Service
 185:         $request = DataRequest::create($validated);
 186:         $request->user()->notify(new RequestCreated($request));
 187:         Log::info('Request created', ['id' => $request->id]);
 188:     });
 189: }
 190: ```
 191: 
 192: ### Services: Business Logic Orchestration
 193: ```php
 194: // ✓ Service orchestrates multiple steps, manages transactions
 195: class DataRequestService
 196: {
 197:     public function __construct(
 198:         private DataRequestRepository $repository,
 199:         private NotificationService $notifications
 200:     ) {}
 201: 
 202:     public function createRequest(User $user, array $data): DataRequest
 203:     {
 204:         return DB::transaction(function () use ($user, $data) {
 205:             $request = $this->repository->create([
 206:                 'user_id' => $user->id,
 207:                 'status' => RequestStatus::PENDING,
 208:                 ...$data,
 209:             ]);
 210: 
 211:             event(new DataRequestSubmitted($request));
 212:             
 213:             $this->notifications->notifyAdmins($request);
 214: 
 215:             return $request;
 216:         });
 217:     }
 218: 
 219:     public function approveRequest(DataRequest $request, User $approver): bool
 220:     {
 221:         if (!$approver->can('approve', $request)) {
 222:             throw new InsufficientPermissionsException();
 223:         }
 224: 
 225:         return DB::transaction(function () use ($request, $approver) {
 226:             $updated = $request->update([
 227:                 'status' => RequestStatus::APPROVED,
 228:                 'approved_by' => $approver->id,
 229:                 'approved_at' => now(),
 230:             ]);
 231: 
 232:             event(new DataRequestApproved($request));
 233: 
 234:             return $updated;
 235:         });
 236:     }
 237: }
 238: ```
 239: 
 240: ### Actions: Single Responsibility
 241: ```php
 242: // ✓ Action does ONE thing well, highly focused
 243: class CreateUser
 244: {
 245:     public function handle(array $data): User
 246:     {
 247:         return User::create([
 248:             'name' => $data['name'],
 249:             'email' => $data['email'],
 250:             'password' => Hash::make($data['password']),
 251:             'email_verified_at' => $data['verify_email'] ? now() : null,
 252:         ]);
 253:     }
 254: }
 255: 
 256: // Usage in Service
 257: class UserService
 258: {
 259:     public function registerUser(array $data): User
 260:     {
 261:         return DB::transaction(function () use ($data) {
 262:             $user = app(CreateUser::class)->handle($data);
 263:             
 264:             event(new UserRegistered($user));
 265:             
 266:             return $user;
 267:         });
 268:     }
 269: }
 270: ```
 271: 
 272: ### Repositories: Data Access Abstraction
 273: ```php
 274: // Interface
 275: interface UserRepositoryInterface
 276: {
 277:     public function findByEmail(string $email): ?User;
 278:     public function findActiveUsers(): Collection;
 279:     public function create(array $data): User;
 280: }
 281: 
 282: // Implementation
 283: class UserRepository implements UserRepositoryInterface
 284: {
 285:     public function findByEmail(string $email): ?User
 286:     {
 287:         return User::where('email', $email)->first();
 288:     }
 289: 
 290:     public function findActiveUsers(): Collection
 291:     {
 292:         return User::where('is_active', true)
 293:             ->orderBy('name')
 294:             ->get();
 295:     }
 296: 
 297:     public function create(array $data): User
 298:     {
 299:         return User::create($data);
 300:     }
 301: }
 302: 
 303: // Register in AppServiceProvider
 304: public function register(): void
 305: {
 306:     $this->app->bind(
 307:         UserRepositoryInterface::class,
 308:         UserRepository::class
 309:     );
 310: }
 311: ```
 312: 
 313: ### Policies: Authorization Logic
 314: ```php
 315: // ✓ Extract authorization to Policy
 316: class DataRequestPolicy
 317: {
 318:     public function view(User $user, DataRequest $request): bool
 319:     {
 320:         return $user->id === $request->user_id || $user->isAdmin();
 321:     }
 322: 
 323:     public function update(User $user, DataRequest $request): bool
 324:     {
 325:         return $user->id === $request->user_id 
 326:             && $request->status === RequestStatus::PENDING;
 327:     }
 328: 
 329:     public function approve(User $user, DataRequest $request): bool
 330:     {
 331:         return $user->isAdmin() && $request->status === RequestStatus::PENDING;
 332:     }
 333: 
 334:     public function delete(User $user, DataRequest $request): bool
 335:     {
 336:         return $user->id === $request->user_id || $user->isAdmin();
 337:     }
 338: }
 339: 
 340: // Register in AuthServiceProvider
 341: protected $policies = [
 342:     DataRequest::class => DataRequestPolicy::class,
 343: ];
 344: 
 345: // Use in controller
 346: public function update(UpdateDataRequestRequest $request, DataRequest $dataRequest)
 347: {
 348:     $this->authorize('update', $dataRequest);
 349:     // ...
 350: }
 351: ```
 352: 
 353: ---
 354: 
 355: ## Decision Framework: When to Use What
 356: 
 357: ### Service vs Action vs Repository
 358: 
 359: **Use SERVICE when:**
 360: - ✓ Orchestrating multiple steps/actions
 361: - ✓ Business logic that doesn't fit in controller
 362: - ✓ Need transactions across multiple models
 363: - ✓ Coordinating events, notifications, logging
 364: - **Pros**: Centralized logic, testable, reusable across controllers
 365: - **Cons**: Can become god classes if not careful, adds indirection
 366: - **Example**: `UserService::registerUser()` - creates user, sends email, logs event
 367: 
 368: **Use ACTION when:**
 369: - ✓ Single responsibility, does ONE thing
 370: - ✓ Complex operation extracted from controller
 371: - ✓ Composable, reusable building blocks
 372: - **Pros**: Highly focused, easy to test, composable in services
 373: - **Cons**: Many small files, navigation complexity
 374: - **Example**: `CreateUser`, `SendWelcomeEmail`, `GenerateReport`
 375: 
 376: **Use REPOSITORY when:**
 377: - ✓ Need to abstract data source (testability)
 378: - ✓ Complex queries repeated across services
 379: - ✓ May swap database/cache/API in future
 380: - **Pros**: Swappable data sources, query reuse, easier mocking
 381: - **Cons**: Boilerplate, over-abstraction risk for simple CRUD
 382: - **Example**: `UserRepository::findActiveByRole()`, `DataRequestRepository::getPendingRequests()`
 383: 
 384: **Decision Matrix:**
 385: ```
 386: Simple CRUD, no logic          → Controller + FormRequest
 387: Business logic, 2-3 steps      → Controller + Service
 388: Complex operation, 1 thing     → Controller + Action
 389: Reusable queries               → Repository
 390: Multiple models, transactions  → Service (may use Actions + Repositories)
 391: ```
 392: 
 393: ### FormRequest vs Inline Validation
 394: 
 395: **Use FORMREQUEST when:**
 396: - ✓ Complex validation (4+ fields, custom rules)
 397: - ✓ Custom validation logic needed
 398: - ✓ Authorization checks required
 399: - ✓ Reusable across multiple methods
 400: - **Pros**: Reusable, testable, clean controllers, co-locates authorization
 401: - **Cons**: Extra file, indirection
 402: - **Example**: [ProfileUpdateRequest.php](app/Http/Requests/ProfileUpdateRequest.php)
 403: 
 404: **Use INLINE when:**
 405: - ✓ Simple validation (1-3 fields)
 406: - ✓ One-off, not reused
 407: - ✓ Quick prototyping
 408: - **Pros**: Less files, validation obvious in controller
 409: - **Cons**: Not reusable, clutters controller, harder to test
 410: - **Example**: `$request->validate(['email' => 'required|email'])`
 411: 
 412: ### Events/Listeners vs Direct Calls
 413: 
 414: **Use EVENTS when:**
 415: - ✓ Multiple systems need to react (email + log + analytics)
 416: - ✓ Decoupling required
 417: - ✓ Async processing possible (queued listeners)
 418: - ✓ Future extensibility (new listeners without changing code)
 419: - **Pros**: Loose coupling, parallel execution, scalable, testable
 420: - **Cons**: Harder to trace execution flow, debugging complexity
 421: - **Example**: `UserRegistered` → [`SendWelcomeEmail`, `LogRegistration`, `NotifyAdmin`]
 422: 
 423: **Use DIRECT CALLS when:**
 424: - ✓ Single action, immediate response required
 425: - ✓ Need to handle result/errors immediately
 426: - ✓ Tight coupling acceptable
 427: - **Pros**: Simple, traceable, predictable flow, immediate error handling
 428: - **Cons**: Tight coupling, harder to extend, not async
 429: - **Example**: `$user->notify(new WelcomeNotification($user))`
 430: 
 431: **When to ask user:**
 432: - First event in project (establish pattern)
 433: - Operation that MAY need multiple reactions in future
 434: 
 435: ### Caching Strategy
 436: 
 437: **Cache QUERIES when:**
 438: - ✓ Data changes infrequently (config, settings, categories)
 439: - ✓ Expensive queries (complex joins, aggregations)
 440: - ✓ High read/low write ratio
 441: - **Pros**: Significant performance gain, reduced DB load
 442: - **Cons**: Cache invalidation complexity, stale data risk, memory usage
 443: ```php
 444: Cache::remember('active-categories', 3600, fn() => Category::active()->get());
 445: ```
 446: 
 447: **Cache ROUTES when:**
 448: - ✓ Production environment only
 449: - ✓ No closure-based routes
 450: - **Pros**: Faster route resolution
 451: - **Cons**: Must clear on deploy, not for development
 452: ```bash
 453: php artisan route:cache  # Production only
 454: ```
 455: 
 456: **DON'T cache:**
 457: - ✗ User-specific data (unless keyed by user ID)
 458: - ✗ Frequently changing data
 459: - ✗ Development environment
 460: 
 461: ### Frontend State Management
 462: 
 463: **INERTIA PROPS (Server State):**
 464: - ✓ User data, permissions, initial page data
 465: - ✓ Data from database
 466: - **Pros**: SSR-friendly, fresh on page load, no sync issues
 467: - **Cons**: Full page visit to update, larger initial payload
 468: 
 469: **LOCAL STATE (useState):**
 470: - ✓ Form inputs, UI toggles, modal state
 471: - ✓ Component-scoped temporary data
 472: - **Pros**: Simple, no setup, perfect for UI state
 473: - **Cons**: Lost on navigation, can't share between components
 474: 
 475: **GLOBAL STATE (Context/Zustand):**
 476: - ✓ Client-only data (theme, sidebar state, toast notifications)
 477: - ✓ Shared across components without navigation
 478: - **Pros**: Persists across pages, shareable
 479: - **Cons**: Not SSR-friendly, sync with server tricky, complexity
 480: 
 481: **Decision:** Prefer Inertia props → Local state → Global state (only when necessary)
 482: 
 483: ---
 484: 
 485: ## Security Requirements
 486: 
 487: ### REQUIRED Security Middleware
 488: ```php
 489: // app/Http/Middleware/SecurityHeaders.php - CREATE THIS
 490: class SecurityHeaders
 491: {
 492:     public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next): Response
 493:     {
 494:         $response = $next($request);
 495: 
 496:         return $response->withHeaders([
 497:             'X-Content-Type-Options' => 'nosniff',
 498:             'X-Frame-Options' => 'SAMEORIGIN',
 499:             'X-XSS-Protection' => '1; mode=block',
 500:             'Referrer-Policy' => 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin',
 501:             'Permissions-Policy' => 'geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()',
 502:             // Add HSTS in production:
 503:             // 'Strict-Transport-Security' => 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains',
 504:         ]);
 505:     }
 506: }
 507: 
 508: // Register in bootstrap/app.php
 509: ->withMiddleware(function (Middleware $middleware) {
 510:     $middleware->append(SecurityHeaders::class);
 511: })
 512: ```
 513: 
 514: ### Input Validation & Sanitization
 515: ```php
 516: // ✓ ALWAYS validate input
 517: class StoreDataRequestRequest extends FormRequest
 518: {
 519:     public function rules(): array
 520:     {
 521:         return [
 522:             'title' => ['required', 'string', 'max:255'],
 523:             'description' => ['required', 'string', 'max:1000'],
 524:             'email' => ['required', 'email:rfc,dns'], // Strict validation
 525:             'data_type' => ['required', 'in:meteorology,geophysics,maritime'],
 526:             'date_from' => ['required', 'date', 'before_or_equal:date_to'],
 527:             'date_to' => ['required', 'date', 'after_or_equal:date_from'],
 528:         ];
 529:     }
 530: 
 531:     public function messages(): array
 532:     {
 533:         return [
 534:             'data_type.in' => 'Invalid data type selected.',
 535:             'date_to.after_or_equal' => 'End date must be after start date.',
 536:         ];
 537:     }
 538: }
 539: 
 540: // ✗ NEVER trust user input
 541: $request->input('email'); // Must validate first
 542: $request->all(); // Validate before using
 543: 
 544: // ✓ For rich text, sanitize HTML
 545: use Illuminate\Support\Str;
 546: $clean = Str::of($request->description)->stripTags()->trim();
 547: ```
 548: 
 549: ### Authorization Checks
 550: ```php
 551: // ✓ ALWAYS check authorization
 552: public function update(UpdateDataRequestRequest $request, DataRequest $dataRequest)
 553: {
 554:     $this->authorize('update', $dataRequest); // Uses Policy
 555:     
 556:     // Or inline:
 557:     if (!$request->user()->can('update', $dataRequest)) {
 558:         abort(403);
 559:     }
 560: }
 561: 
 562: // ✓ Check in Inertia responses
 563: public function edit(DataRequest $dataRequest)
 564: {
 565:     $this->authorize('update', $dataRequest);
 566:     
 567:     return Inertia::render('DataRequests/Edit', [
 568:         'dataRequest' => $dataRequest,
 569:         'canDelete' => $request->user()->can('delete', $dataRequest),
 570:     ]);
 571: }
 572: ```
 573: 
 574: ### CSRF Protection
 575: - ✓ Already configured via [bootstrap.js](resources/js/bootstrap.js) - axios sends `X-CSRF-TOKEN`
 576: - ✓ All POST/PUT/DELETE routes automatically protected
 577: - ✗ Never disable CSRF for web routes
 578: 
 579: ### Rate Limiting
 580: ```php
 581: // Apply to sensitive routes
 582: Route::middleware(['throttle:10,1'])->group(function () {
 583:     Route::post('/data-requests', [DataRequestController::class, 'store']);
 584: });
 585: 
 586: // In FormRequest (like [LoginRequest.php](app/Http/Requests/Auth/LoginRequest.php#L60-L75))
 587: RateLimiter::hit($this->throttleKey(), 60); // 1 minute lockout
 588: ```
 589: 
 590: ### SQL Injection Prevention
 591: ```php
 592: // ✓ ALWAYS use parameter binding
 593: User::where('email', $email)->first(); // Eloquent auto-binds
 594: DB::select('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?', [$email]); // Manual binding
 595: 
 596: // ✗ NEVER raw SQL with user input
 597: DB::select("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '$email'"); // VULNERABLE!
 598: ```
 599: 
 600: ### Mass Assignment Protection
 601: ```php
 602: // ✓ Define $fillable or $guarded
 603: class DataRequest extends Model
 604: {
 605:     protected $fillable = [
 606:         'user_id',
 607:         'title',
 608:         'description',
 609:         'data_type',
 610:         'status',
 611:     ];
 612: 
 613:     // Never expose sensitive fields
 614:     protected $hidden = ['internal_notes', 'admin_comments'];
 615: }
 616: ```
 617: 
 618: ### Environment Variables
 619: ```php
 620: // ✓ NEVER commit .env
 621: // ✓ Keep sensitive data in .env
 622: 'api_key' => env('BMKG_API_KEY'),
 623: 
 624: // ✓ Production settings
 625: APP_DEBUG=false
 626: APP_ENV=production
 627: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true
 628: SESSION_SAME_SITE=strict
 629: ```
 630: 
 631: ---
 632: 
 633: ## Code Organization (DRY Principles)
 634: 
 635: ### NEVER Repeat Yourself
 636: ```php
 637: // ✗ BAD: Duplicated query logic
 638: class DataRequestController {
 639:     public function index() {
 640:         $requests = DataRequest::where('status', 'pending')
 641:             ->where('user_id', auth()->id())
 642:             ->orderBy('created_at', 'desc')
 643:             ->get();
 644:     }
 645:     
 646:     public function dashboard() {
 647:         $requests = DataRequest::where('status', 'pending')
 648:             ->where('user_id', auth()->id())
 649:             ->orderBy('created_at', 'desc')
 650:             ->get();
 651:     }
 652: }
 653: 
 654: // ✓ GOOD: Extract to scope or repository
 655: class DataRequest extends Model {
 656:     public function scopePendingForUser($query, User $user)
 657:     {
 658:         return $query->where('status', 'pending')
 659:             ->where('user_id', $user->id)
 660:             ->orderBy('created_at', 'desc');
 661:     }
 662: }
 663: 
 664: // Usage
 665: $requests = DataRequest::pendingForUser(auth()->user())->get();
 666: ```
 667: 
 668: ### Extract Shared Logic
 669: ```php
 670: // ✗ BAD: Repeated validation in multiple requests
 671: class StoreRequestA { rules() { return ['email' => 'required|email']; } }
 672: class StoreRequestB { rules() { return ['email' => 'required|email']; } }
 673: 
 674: // ✓ GOOD: Trait or base class
 675: trait HasEmailValidation {
 676:     protected function emailRules(): array {
 677:         return ['email' => ['required', 'email:rfc,dns', 'max:255']];
 678:     }
 679: }
 680: ```
 681: 
 682: ### Reusable Components
 683: ```jsx
 684: // ✗ BAD: Copying component code
 685: <input className="border-gray-300 focus:border-indigo-500..." />
 686: 
 687: // ✓ GOOD: Reusable component
 688: <TextInput /> // From [Components/TextInput.jsx](resources/js/Components/TextInput.jsx)
 689: ```
 690: 
 691: ---
 692: 
 693: ## Testing Standards
 694: 
 695: ### Test-First Development (MANDATORY)
 696: 
 697: **ALWAYS write tests for new features BEFORE or WITH implementation. No feature is complete without tests.**
 698: 
 699: ### When Tests Are REQUIRED (Non-Negotiable)
 700: 
 701: ✅ **MUST write tests for:**
 702: - **All new features** (controllers, services, actions, repositories)
 703: - **Bug fixes** (write failing test first, then fix, test passes)
 704: - **Business logic** (calculations, workflows, data transformations)
 705: - **Authentication/Authorization** (login, permissions, policies)
 706: - **Data mutations** (create, update, delete operations)
 707: - **API endpoints** (if added later)
 708: - **Critical user flows** (registration, data requests, payments)
 709: 
 710: ### When Tests Are OPTIONAL
 711: 
 712: 📝 **May skip tests for:**
 713: - **Trivial getters/setters** (already tested through feature tests)
 714: - **View-only pages** (no logic, just display)
 715: - **UI styling changes** (Tailwind classes, layout adjustments)
 716: - **Quick prototypes** (MUST add tests before merging to main branch)
 717: 
 718: ### Required Test Coverage
 719: - **Feature tests**: All controller actions, happy path + error cases  
 720: - **Unit tests**: Services, Actions, Repositories (isolate business logic)
 721: - **Minimum coverage**: 70% (aim for 80%+)
 722: - **Before commit**: Run `composer run test` - all tests MUST pass
 723: 
 724: ### Test Development Workflow
 725: 
 726: **For Every New Feature:**
 727: ```
 728: 1. Write failing test first (red)
 729: 2. Implement minimum code to pass (green)
 730: 3. Refactor while keeping tests passing (refactor)
 731: 4. Run full test suite before commit
 732: ```
 733: 
 734: **Example: Adding Data Request Feature**
 735: ```
 736: Step 1: Create test/Feature/DataRequestTest.php with failing tests
 737: Step 2: Create controller method (tests still fail)
 738: Step 3: Add service logic (tests start passing)
 739: Step 4: Run `composer run test` (all green ✓)
 740: Step 5: Commit with message: "feat: add data request submission with tests"
 741: ```
 742: 
 743: ### Feature Test Pattern
 744: ```php
 745: use Illuminate\Foundation\Testing\RefreshDatabase;
 746: 
 747: class DataRequestTest extends TestCase
 748: {
 749:     use RefreshDatabase;
 750: 
 751:     public function test_authenticated_user_can_create_data_request(): void
 752:     {
 753:         $user = User::factory()->create();
 754: 
 755:         $response = $this
 756:             ->actingAs($user)
 757:             ->post(route('data-requests.store'), [
 758:                 'title' => 'Weather Data Request',
 759:                 'description' => 'Need temperature data',
 760:                 'data_type' => 'meteorology',
 761:                 'date_from' => '2026-01-01',
 762:                 'date_to' => '2026-01-31',
 763:             ]);
 764: 
 765:         $response->assertRedirect(route('data-requests.index'));
 766:         $response->assertSessionHasNoErrors();
 767:         
 768:         $this->assertDatabaseHas('data_requests', [
 769:             'user_id' => $user->id,
 770:             'title' => 'Weather Data Request',
 771:             'status' => 'pending',
 772:         ]);
 773:     }
 774: 
 775:     public function test_guest_cannot_create_data_request(): void
 776:     {
 777:         $response = $this->post(route('data-requests.store'), [
 778:             'title' => 'Test',
 779:         ]);
 780: 
 781:         $response->assertRedirect(route('login'));
 782:     }
 783: 
 784:     public function test_validation_fails_with_invalid_data(): void
 785:     {
 786:         $user = User::factory()->create();
 787: 
 788:         $response = $this
 789:             ->actingAs($user)
 790:             ->post(route('data-requests.store'), [
 791:                 'title' => '', // Invalid
 792:                 'data_type' => 'invalid', // Invalid
 793:             ]);
 794: 
 795:         $response->assertSessionHasErrors(['title', 'data_type']);
 796:     }
 797: }
 798: 
 799: // MINIMUM 3 tests per feature: Happy path, Auth check, Validation
 800: ```
 801: ```
 802: 
 803: ### Unit Test Pattern
 804: ```php
 805: class UserServiceTest extends TestCase
 806: {
 807:     use RefreshDatabase;
 808: 
 809:     public function test_create_user_hashes_password(): void
 810:     {
 811:         $service = new UserService(new UserRepository());
 812: 
 813:         $user = $service->createUser([
 814:             'name' => 'John Doe',
 815:             'email' => 'john@example.com',
 816:             'password' => 'password',
 817:         ]);
 818: 
 819:         $this->assertTrue(Hash::check('password', $user->password));
 820:         $this->assertEquals('john@example.com', $user->email);
 821:     }
 822: }
 823: ```
 824: 
 825: ### Factory Usage
 826: ```php
 827: // ✓ Use factories in tests
 828: $user = User::factory()->create(['email' => 'test@example.com']);
 829: $users = User::factory()->count(10)->create();
 830: $unverifiedUser = User::factory()->unverified()->create();
 831: 
 832: // Define custom states in factories
 833: public function admin(): static
 834: {
 835:     return $this->state(fn (array $attributes) => [
 836:         'role' => 'admin',
 837:         'is_admin' => true,
 838:     ]);
 839: }
 840: ```
 841: 
 842: ### Test Database
 843: ```php
 844: // ✓ Use RefreshDatabase trait
 845: use RefreshDatabase; // Runs migrations, rollback after test
 846: 
 847: // ✓ Or DatabaseTransactions
 848: use DatabaseTransactions; // Wraps in transaction, rollback after
 849: ```
 850: 
 851: ---
 852: 
 853: ## Error Handling
 854: 
 855: ### Custom Exception Handler
 856: ```php
 857: // bootstrap/app.php - UPDATE THIS
 858: ->withExceptions(function (Exceptions $exceptions) {
 859:     $exceptions->respond(function (Response $response, Throwable $exception, Request $request) {
 860:         // Inertia error pages
 861:         if ($request->inertia() && in_array($response->getStatusCode(), [500, 503, 404, 403])) {
 862:             return Inertia::render('Error', [
 863:                 'status' => $response->getStatusCode(),
 864:                 'message' => $exception->getMessage(),
 865:             ])->toResponse($request)->setStatusCode($response->getStatusCode());
 866:         }
 867: 
 868:         return $response;
 869:     });
 870:     
 871:     // Report to logging service (future: Sentry, Bugsnag)
 872:     $exceptions->report(function (Throwable $e) {
 873:         if (app()->environment('production')) {
 874:             // Log::channel('slack')->error($e->getMessage());
 875:         }
 876:     });
 877: })
 878: ```
 879: 
 880: ### Frontend Error Boundary
 881: ```jsx
 882: // resources/js/Components/ErrorBoundary.jsx - CREATE THIS
 883: import React from 'react';
 884: 
 885: export default class ErrorBoundary extends React.Component {
 886:     constructor(props) {
 887:         super(props);
 888:         this.state = { hasError: false, error: null };
 889:     }
 890: 
 891:     static getDerivedStateFromError(error) {
 892:         return { hasError: true, error };
 893:     }
 894: 
 895:     componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) {
 896:         console.error('Error caught by boundary:', error, errorInfo);
 897:         // Future: Send to error tracking service
 898:     }
 899: 
 900:     render() {
 901:         if (this.state.hasError) {
 902:             return (
 903:                 <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center">
 904:                     <div className="text-center">
 905:                         <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold text-red-600">Something went wrong</h1>
 906:                         <p className="mt-2 text-gray-600">{this.state.error?.message}</p>
 907:                         <button
 908:                             onClick={() => window.location.reload()}
 909:                             className="mt-4 px-4 py-2 bg-indigo-600 text-white rounded"
 910:                         >
 911:                             Reload Page
 912:                         </button>
 913:                     </div>
 914:                 </div>
 915:             );
 916:         }
 917: 
 918:         return this.props.children;
 919:     }
 920: }
 921: 
 922: // Wrap app in resources/js/app.jsx
 923: createInertiaApp({
 924:     resolve: (name) => resolvePageComponent(`./Pages/${name}.jsx`, import.meta.glob('./Pages/**/*.jsx')),
 925:     setup({ el, App, props }) {
 926:         createRoot(el).render(
 927:             <ErrorBoundary>
 928:                 <App {...props} />
 929:             </ErrorBoundary>
 930:         );
 931:     },
 932: });
 933: ```
 934: 
 935: ### User-Friendly Error Messages
 936: ```php
 937: // ✓ Custom exception with user message
 938: class InsufficientPermissionsException extends Exception
 939: {
 940:     public function __construct()
 941:     {
 942:         parent::__construct('You do not have permission to perform this action.');
 943:     }
 944:     
 945:     public function render(Request $request)
 946:     {
 947:         return back()->with('error', $this->getMessage());
 948:     }
 949: }
 950: 
 951: // ✓ Validation messages
 952: public function messages(): array
 953: {
 954:     return [
 955:         'email.required' => 'Email address is required.',
 956:         'email.email' => 'Please enter a valid email address.',
 957:         'date_to.after_or_equal' => 'End date must be after or equal to start date.',
 958:     ];
 959: }
 960: ```
 961: 
 962: ---
 963: 
 964: ## Database Best Practices
 965: 
 966: ### Query Optimization
 967: ```php
 968: // ✗ N+1 Problem
 969: $requests = DataRequest::all();
 970: foreach ($requests as $request) {
 971:     echo $request->user->name; // Triggers query for each request
 972: }
 973: 
 974: // ✓ Eager Loading
 975: $requests = DataRequest::with('user')->get();
 976: foreach ($requests as $request) {
 977:     echo $request->user->name; // No additional queries
 978: }
 979: 
 980: // ✓ Load only needed columns
 981: DataRequest::select('id', 'title', 'status')->get();
 982: 
 983: // ✓ Chunk for large datasets
 984: DataRequest::chunk(200, function ($requests) {
 985:     foreach ($requests as $request) {
 986:         // Process
 987:     }
 988: });
 989: ```
 990: 
 991: ### Indexes
 992: ```php
 993: // ✓ Add indexes for foreign keys, frequently queried columns
 994: Schema::create('data_requests', function (Blueprint $table) {
 995:     $table->id();
 996:     $table->foreignId('user_id')->constrained()->onDelete('cascade');
 997:     $table->string('status')->index(); // Frequently filtered
 998:     $table->string('email')->index(); // Frequently searched
 999:     $table->timestamps();
1000:     
1001:     // Composite index for common query combinations
1002:     $table->index(['user_id', 'status']);
1003: });
1004: ```
1005: 
1006: ### Transactions
1007: ```php
1008: // ✓ ALWAYS use transactions for multi-step operations
1009: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
1010: 
1011: DB::transaction(function () use ($data) {
1012:     $request = DataRequest::create($data);
1013:     $request->attachments()->createMany($data['attachments']);
1014:     $request->user->increment('request_count');
1015: });
1016: 
1017: // ✓ Or manually
1018: DB::beginTransaction();
1019: try {
1020:     // Operations
1021:     DB::commit();
1022: } catch (\Exception $e) {
1023:     DB::rollBack();
1024:     throw $e;
1025: }
1026: ```
1027: 
1028: ### Model Scopes
1029: ```php
1030: // ✓ Reusable query logic
1031: class DataRequest extends Model
1032: {
1033:     public function scopePending($query)
1034:     {
1035:         return $query->where('status', 'pending');
1036:     }
1037: 
1038:     public function scopeForUser($query, User $user)
1039:     {
1040:         return $query->where('user_id', $user->id);
1041:     }
1042: 
1043:     public function scopeRecent($query)
1044:     {
1045:         return $query->orderBy('created_at', 'desc');
1046:     }
1047: }
1048: 
1049: // Usage: Chain scopes
1050: DataRequest::pending()->forUser($user)->recent()->get();
1051: ```
1052: 
1053: ---
1054: 
1055: ## Frontend Standards
1056: 
1057: ### Component Best Practices
1058: ```jsx
1059: // ✓ GOOD: forwardRef pattern from [TextInput.jsx](resources/js/Components/TextInput.jsx)
1060: import { forwardRef, useEffect, useImperativeHandle, useRef } from 'react';
1061: 
1062: export default forwardRef(function TextInput(
1063:     { type = 'text', className = '', isFocused = false, ...props },
1064:     ref
1065: ) {
1066:     const localRef = useRef(null);
1067: 
1068:     useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
1069:         focus: () => localRef.current?.focus(),
1070:     }));
1071: 
1072:     useEffect(() => {
1073:         if (isFocused) {
1074:             localRef.current?.focus();
1075:         }
1076:     }, [isFocused]);
1077: 
1078:     return (
1079:         <input
1080:             {...props}
1081:             type={type}
1082:             className={'border-gray-300 focus:border-indigo-500 focus:ring-indigo-500 rounded-md shadow-sm ' + className}
1083:             ref={localRef}
1084:         />
1085:     );
1086: });
1087: ```
1088: 
1089: ### Page Structure & Modularization
1090: 
1091: **Pattern: Composition over Monolithic Pages**
1092: 
1093: Follow Next.js-style modularization: break down pages into smaller, focused components. Keep page files thin (~50-100 lines) by extracting sections into Partials or reusable Components.
1094: 
1095: ```
1096: resources/js/Pages/
1097: └── [Feature]/
1098:     ├── Index.jsx              # Thin composition page (50-100 lines)
1099:     ├── Create.jsx
1100:     ├── Edit.jsx
1101:     ├── Show.jsx
1102:     └── Partials/              # Page-specific components
1103:         ├── [Feature]List.jsx
1104:         ├── [Feature]Filters.jsx
1105:         ├── [Feature]Stats.jsx
1106:         └── [Feature]Form.jsx
1107: 
1108: resources/js/Components/       # Shared/reusable components
1109:     ├── DataTable.jsx
1110:     ├── PrimaryButton.jsx
1111:     └── Modal.jsx
1112: ```
1113: 
1114: **When to create Partials/ (Page-Specific Components):**
1115: - ✓ Component used **only in this specific page**
1116: - ✓ Tightly coupled to page's data structure
1117: - ✓ Contains page-specific business logic
1118: - ✓ Improves page readability (keeps page < 100 lines)
1119: - **Examples**: `RequestFilters.jsx`, `ProfileEditForm.jsx`, `DashboardStats.jsx`
1120: 
1121: **When to use Components/ (Reusable Components):**
1122: - ✓ Component is **reusable across 2+ pages**
1123: - ✓ Generic/abstract functionality
1124: - ✓ No page-specific logic
1125: - ✓ Design system components
1126: - **Examples**: `PrimaryButton.jsx`, `Modal.jsx`, `DataTable.jsx`, `TextInput.jsx`
1127: 
1128: **Example: Thin Page Composition**
1129: ```jsx
1130: // ✓ GOOD: Modular page (thin composition ~60 lines)
1131: // resources/js/Pages/DataRequests/Index.jsx
1132: import AuthenticatedLayout from '@/Layouts/AuthenticatedLayout';
1133: import RequestStats from './Partials/RequestStats';
1134: import RequestFilters from './Partials/RequestFilters';
1135: import RequestList from './Partials/RequestList';
1136: import { Head } from '@inertiajs/react';
1137: 
1138: export default function Index({ auth, requests, stats, filters }) {
1139:     return (
1140:         <AuthenticatedLayout
1141:             user={auth.user}
1142:             header={<h2 className="font-semibold text-xl">Data Requests</h2>}
1143:         >
1144:             <Head title="Data Requests" />
1145: 
1146:             <div className="py-12">
1147:                 <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto sm:px-6 lg:px-8 space-y-6">
1148:                     <RequestStats stats={stats} />
1149:                     <RequestFilters initialFilters={filters} />
1150:                     <RequestList requests={requests} />
1151:                 </div>
1152:             </div>
1153:         </AuthenticatedLayout>
1154:     );
1155: }
1156: 
1157: // ✗ BAD: Monolithic page (400+ lines)
1158: export default function Index({ auth, requests }) {
1159:     return (
1160:         <Layout>
1161:             {/* 400 lines of stats, filters, list JSX here */}
1162:         </Layout>
1163:     );
1164: }
1165: ```
1166: 
1167: **Example: Page-Specific Partial**
1168: ```jsx
1169: // resources/js/Pages/DataRequests/Partials/RequestList.jsx
1170: import DataTable from '@/Components/DataTable'; // Shared component
1171: import { Link } from '@inertiajs/react';
1172: 
1173: export default function RequestList({ requests }) {
1174:     return (
1175:         <div className="bg-white shadow-sm rounded-lg">
1176:             <DataTable
1177:                 data={requests}
1178:                 columns={[
1179:                     { key: 'title', label: 'Title' },
1180:                     { key: 'status', label: 'Status' },
1181:                     { key: 'created_at', label: 'Date' },
1182:                 ]}
1183:                 renderRow={(request) => (
1184:                     <tr key={request.id}>
1185:                         <td className="px-6 py-4">{request.title}</td>
1186:                         <td className="px-6 py-4">
1187:                             <StatusBadge status={request.status} />
1188:                         </td>
1189:                         <td className="px-6 py-4">{formatDate(request.created_at)}</td>
1190:                         <td className="px-6 py-4">
1191:                             <Link
1192:                                 href={route('data-requests.show', request.id)}
1193:                                 className="text-indigo-600 hover:text-indigo-900"
1194:                             >
1195:                                 View
1196:                             </Link>
1197:                         </td>
1198:                     </tr>
1199:                 )}
1200:             />
1201:         </div>
1202:     );
1203: }
1204: 
1205: // Local component (only used in this partial, not extracted)
1206: function StatusBadge({ status }) {
1207:     const colors = {
1208:         pending: 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800',
1209:         approved: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800',
1210:         rejected: 'bg-red-100 text-red-800',
1211:     };
1212: 
1213:     return (
1214:         <span className={`px-2 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${colors[status]}`}>
1215:             {status}
1216:         </span>
1217:     );
1218: }
1219: ```
1220: 
1221: **Example: Reusable Shared Component**
1222: ```jsx
1223: // resources/js/Components/DataTable.jsx
1224: // Generic table used across multiple pages
1225: export default function DataTable({ data, columns, renderRow }) {
1226:     if (!data || data.length === 0) {
1227:         return (
1228:             <div className="text-center py-12 text-gray-500">
1229:                 No data available
1230:             </div>
1231:         );
1232:     }
1233: 
1234:     return (
1235:         <table className="min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200">
1236:             <thead className="bg-gray-50">
1237:                 <tr>
1238:                     {columns.map((col) => (
1239:                         <th
1240:                             key={col.key}
1241:                             className="px-6 py-3 text-left text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider"
1242:                         >
1243:                             {col.label}
1244:                         </th>
1245:                     ))}
1246:                 </tr>
1247:             </thead>
1248:             <tbody className="bg-white divide-y divide-gray-200">
1249:                 {data.map(renderRow)}
1250:             </tbody>
1251:         </table>
1252:     );
1253: }
1254: ```
1255: 
1256: **Benefits of Modular Structure:**
1257: - ✓ **Maintainability**: Easy to locate and update specific sections
1258: - ✓ **Readability**: Page files stay thin (~50-100 lines), easy to understand flow
1259: - ✓ **Reusability**: Can extract Partials → Components when needed elsewhere
1260: - ✓ **Collaboration**: Multiple developers work on same page without conflicts
1261: - ✓ **Testing**: Test individual partials in isolation
1262: - ✓ **Code Splitting**: Vite can optimize bundle size automatically
1263: - ✓ **Familiarity**: Follows Next.js conventions (page.tsx with components)
1264: 
1265: **When to Modularize:**
1266: - ✓ Page exceeds 100 lines
1267: - ✓ Distinct sections (stats, filters, list, form)
1268: - ✓ Section has complex logic (can be tested separately)
1269: - ✓ Section might be reused elsewhere in future
1270: 
1271: **When NOT to Modularize:**
1272: - ✗ Page is simple (< 50 lines total)
1273: - ✗ No clear section boundaries
1274: - ✗ Over-abstraction (1 component per 10 lines is overkill)
1275: 
1276: **Extracting Partials to Components:**
1277: When a Partial becomes useful elsewhere, promote it:
1278: ```bash
1279: # Move from page-specific to shared
1280: mv resources/js/Pages/DataRequests/Partials/StatusBadge.jsx \
1281:    resources/js/Components/StatusBadge.jsx
1282: 
1283: # Update imports
1284: # From: import StatusBadge from './Partials/StatusBadge';
1285: # To:   import StatusBadge from '@/Components/StatusBadge';
1286: ```
1287: 
1288: ### Form Handling with Inertia
1289: ```jsx
1290: // ✓ Pattern from [Login.jsx](resources/js/Pages/Auth/Login.jsx)
1291: import { useForm } from '@inertiajs/react';
1292: 
1293: export default function CreateDataRequest() {
1294:     const { data, setData, post, processing, errors, reset } = useForm({
1295:         title: '',
1296:         description: '',
1297:         data_type: '',
1298:     });
1299: 
1300:     const submit = (e) => {
1301:         e.preventDefault();
1302:         post(route('data-requests.store'), {
1303:             onSuccess: () => reset(),
1304:             onError: () => {
1305:                 // Form errors automatically populate in `errors`
1306:             },
1307:         });
1308:     };
1309: 
1310:     return (
1311:         <form onSubmit={submit}>
1312:             <TextInput
1313:                 value={data.title}
1314:                 onChange={(e) => setData('title', e.target.value)}
1315:             />
1316:             <InputError message={errors.title} />
1317:             
1318:             <PrimaryButton disabled={processing}>
1319:                 {processing ? 'Creating...' : 'Create Request'}
1320:             </PrimaryButton>
1321:         </form>
1322:     );
1323: }
1324: ```
1325: 
1326: ### Custom Hooks (Create These)
1327: ```jsx
1328: // resources/js/Hooks/useAuth.js
1329: export function useAuth() {
1330:     const { auth } = usePage().props;
1331:     return {
1332:         user: auth.user,
1333:         isAuthenticated: !!auth.user,
1334:         isAdmin: auth.user?.role === 'admin',
1335:     };
1336: }
1337: 
1338: // resources/js/Hooks/useDebounce.js
1339: import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
1340: 
1341: export function useDebounce(value, delay = 500) {
1342:     const [debouncedValue, setDebouncedValue] = useState(value);
1343: 
1344:     useEffect(() => {
1345:         const handler = setTimeout(() => setDebouncedValue(value), delay);
1346:         return () => clearTimeout(handler);
1347:     }, [value, delay]);
1348: 
1349:     return debouncedValue;
1350: }
1351: 
1352: // Usage
1353: const searchTerm = useDebounce(data.search, 300);
1354: ```
1355: 
1356: ### PropTypes/JSDoc Comments
1357: ```jsx
1358: // ✓ Document component props
1359: /**
1360:  * Primary button component for main actions
1361:  * @param {Object} props
1362:  * @param {React.ReactNode} props.children - Button content
1363:  * @param {string} [props.className] - Additional CSS classes
1364:  * @param {boolean} [props.disabled] - Whether button is disabled
1365:  * @param {Function} props.onClick - Click handler
1366:  */
1367: export default function PrimaryButton({ children, className = '', disabled = false, ...props }) {
1368:     // Component implementation
1369: }
1370: ```
1371: 
1372: ### Loading States
1373: ```jsx
1374: // ✓ Show loading states
1375: {processing && <div className="spinner">Loading...</div>}
1376: 
1377: // ✓ Disable during submission
1378: <PrimaryButton disabled={processing}>
1379:     {processing ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}
1380: </PrimaryButton>
1381: 
1382: // ✓ Skeleton loaders for initial page load (future enhancement)
1383: ```
1384: 
1385: ### Utils & Constants Organization
1386: 
1387: **Utils vs Constants vs Tailwind Config:**
1388: 
1389: | What | Where | When to Use |
1390: |------|-------|-------------|
1391: | **Colors/Typography for CSS** | `tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind classes (`bg-bmkg-primary`) |
1392: | **Colors for JS logic** | `Constants/config.js` | Used in conditions, not styling |
1393: | **Status enums** | `Constants/statuses.js` | Avoid magic strings, labels |
1394: | **App config** | `Constants/config.js` | File limits, pagination, delays |
1395: | **Formatters** | `Utils/formatters.js` | Date, currency, file size formatting |
1396: | **Validators** | `Utils/validators.js` | Email, phone, file type validation |
1397: | **Helpers** | `Utils/helpers.js` | General utilities (cn, truncate) |
1398: 
1399: **Design System in Tailwind (Recommended):**
1400: ```js
1401: // tailwind.config.js
1402: export default {
1403:     theme: {
1404:         extend: {
1405:             colors: {
1406:                 bmkg: {
1407:                     primary: '#1E40AF',    // Use as: bg-bmkg-primary
1408:                     secondary: '#4F46E5',
1409:                     accent: '#0EA5E9',
1410:                 },
1411:             },
1412:             fontFamily: {
1413:                 sans: ['Inter var', 'sans-serif'],
1414:             },
1415:         },
1416:     },
1417: };
1418: ```
1419: 
1420: **Constants for JS Logic:**
1421: ```js
1422: // resources/js/Constants/statuses.js
1423: export const REQUEST_STATUS = {
1424:     PENDING: 'pending',
1425:     APPROVED: 'approved',
1426:     REJECTED: 'rejected',
1427: };
1428: 
1429: export const REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS = {
1430:     [REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING]: 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800',
1431:     [REQUEST_STATUS.APPROVED]: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800',
1432:     [REQUEST_STATUS.REJECTED]: 'bg-red-100 text-red-800',
1433: };
1434: ```
1435: 
1436: **Utils for Reusable Functions:**
1437: ```js
1438: // resources/js/Utils/formatters.js
1439: export function formatDate(date, format = 'short') {
1440:     const d = new Date(date);
1441:     return d.toLocaleDateString('id-ID', {
1442:         year: 'numeric',
1443:         month: format === 'short' ? 'short' : 'long',
1444:         day: 'numeric',
1445:     });
1446: }
1447: 
1448: export function formatCurrency(amount) {
1449:     return new Intl.NumberFormat('id-ID', {
1450:         style: 'currency',
1451:         currency: 'IDR',
1452:         minimumFractionDigits: 0,
1453:     }).format(amount);
1454: }
1455: ```
1456: 
1457: **Usage in Components:**
1458: ```jsx
1459: import { REQUEST_STATUS, REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS } from '@/Constants';
1460: import { formatDate } from '@/Utils/formatters';
1461: 
1462: function RequestCard({ request }) {
1463:     return (
1464:         <div className="bg-white p-4 rounded-lg">
1465:             <h3>{request.title}</h3>
1466:             <p className="text-gray-500">{formatDate(request.created_at)}</p>
1467:             <span className={REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS[request.status]}>
1468:                 {request.status}
1469:             </span>
1470:         </div>
1471:     );
1472: }
1473: ```
1474: 
1475: **See [resources/js/theme.md](resources/js/theme.md) for detailed design system documentation.**
1476: 
1477: ---
1478: 
1479: ## Performance & Scalability
1480: 
1481: ### Query Optimization
1482: - ✓ Use `select()` to limit columns
1483: - ✓ Eager load with `with()` to prevent N+1
1484: - ✓ Use `chunk()` for large datasets
1485: - ✓ Add database indexes on foreign keys and filtered columns
1486: 
1487: ### Caching
1488: ```php
1489: // ✓ Cache expensive queries
1490: $categories = Cache::remember('categories', 3600, function () {
1491:     return Category::with('subcategories')->get();
1492: });
1493: 
1494: // ✓ Cache tags for grouped invalidation
1495: Cache::tags(['data-requests'])->put('pending', $pending, 600);
1496: Cache::tags(['data-requests'])->flush(); // Clear all data-request caches
1497: ```
1498: 
1499: ### Queue Long-Running Tasks
1500: ```php
1501: // ✓ Queue emails, exports, heavy processing
1502: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Queue;
1503: 
1504: Queue::push(new ProcessDataExportJob($request));
1505: 
1506: // Or on model events
1507: class DataRequest extends Model {
1508:     protected static function booted() {
1509:         static::created(function ($request) {
1510:             dispatch(new NotifyAdminsJob($request));
1511:         });
1512:     }
1513: }
1514: ```
1515: 
1516: ### Frontend Optimization
1517: - ✓ Lazy load routes: `import.meta.glob('./Pages/**/*.jsx')`
1518: - ✓ Optimize images before upload
1519: - ✓ Use pagination for large lists
1520: - ✓ Debounce search inputs (see `useDebounce` hook above)
1521: 
1522: ---
1523: 
1524: ## Before You Code: Checklist
1525: 
1526: Before implementing a feature, ask yourself:
1527: 
1528: - [ ] **Have you written tests?** → MANDATORY: Write failing tests first (TDD approach)
1529: - [ ] **Is this business logic?** → Consider Service or Action (not Controller)
1530: - [ ] **Is this a database query?** → Check if Repository exists/needed
1531: - [ ] **Is this authorization?** → Should be in Policy
1532: - [ ] **Do multiple systems care?** → Consider Event/Listener
1533: - [ ] **Complex validation?** → Create FormRequest (not inline)
1534: - [ ] **New React component?** → Check [Components/](resources/js/Components) for existing
1535: - [ ] **Test coverage?** → Minimum 3 tests: happy path + auth + validation
1536: - [ ] **User input?** → MUST validate and sanitize
1537: - [ ] **Sensitive operation?** → Check authorization with Policy
1538: - [ ] **Multiple database changes?** → Wrap in transaction
1539: - [ ] **Repeating code?** → Extract to Service/Scope/Hook/Component
1540: - [ ] **All tests passing?** → Run `composer run test` before commit
1541: 
1542: ---
1543: 
1544: ## When to Ask User for Decision
1545: 
1546: AI agents should present options with pros/cons and request user decision for:
1547: 
1548: ### Test Strategy
1549: - **Test approach**: "Write tests first (TDD) or after implementation? TDD pros: better design, catch bugs early. After pros: faster initial development."
1550: - **Test scope**: "Feature tests only or add unit tests for [Service/Action]? Unit tests pros: faster execution, better isolation. Cons: more files."
1551: - **Mock vs Real**: "Mock database or use real transactions? Real pros: confidence. Mock pros: speed."
1552: 
1553: ### Architectural Decisions
1554: - **First Service/Repository/Action**: "I can implement this with [Service/Action/Repository]. Here are the trade-offs..."
1555: - **Event vs Direct Call**: "This could trigger multiple actions. Use Events? Pros: [...] Cons: [...]"
1556: - **State Management**: "This state could be [Inertia Props/Local/Global]. Recommendations: [...]"
1557: 
1558: ### Security Decisions
1559: - **Adding external dependency**: "Package X can help, but adds supply-chain risk. Alternatives: [...]"
1560: - **Changing auth settings**: "This affects all users. Current: [...] Proposed: [...] Risk: [...]"
1561: - **Exposing new API endpoint**: "This creates attack surface. Protection plan: [...]"
1562: 
1563: ### Performance Trade-offs
1564: - **Caching**: "Cache this query? TTL options: [5m/1h/24h]. Invalidation strategy: [...]"
1565: - **Eager loading**: "Load these relations? Reduces queries but increases memory: [...]"
1566: 
1567: ### Major Refactoring
1568: - **Breaking changes**: "This requires updating [X] files. Migration plan: [...]"
1569: - **TypeScript migration**: "Convert to TS? Effort: [high] Benefit: [type safety]. Timeline: [...]"
1570: 
1571: ---
1572: 
1573: ## Architecture Principles
1574: 
1575: ### Inertia SSR - No API Layer
1576: - ✓ All responses use `Inertia::render()` - never return JSON from web routes
1577: - ✓ Shared data via [HandleInertiaRequests](app/Http/Middleware/HandleInertiaRequests.php) middleware
1578: - ✗ No REST API endpoints in `routes/web.php`
1579: 
1580: **Why:** Simplifies architecture, eliminates API versioning, reduces attack surface, type-safe props
1581: 
1582: **Trade-off:** Can't easily add mobile app later (would need separate API routes)
1583: 
1584: ### Session-Based Auth (Not Tokens)
1585: - ✓ Uses database sessions ([config/session.php](config/session.php))
1586: - ✓ CSRF protection enabled
1587: - ✓ Routes defined in [routes/auth.php](routes/auth.php)
1588: 
1589: **Why:** Secure for web apps, Laravel batteries included, no token management
1590: 
1591: **Trade-off:** Not suitable for mobile apps (would need Laravel Sanctum for tokens)
1592: 
1593: ---
1594: 
1595: ## Project Conventions
1596: 
1597: ### Backend
1598: - **Controllers**: Thin (< 50 lines), type-hint return types (`: Response`, `: RedirectResponse`)
1599: - **Services**: Orchestrate business logic, manage transactions, dispatch events
1600: - **Repositories**: Abstract data access, implement interfaces for testability
1601: - **Policies**: All authorization logic (use `$this->authorize()` in controllers)
1602: - **FormRequests**: All validation for 4+ fields or complex rules
1603: - **Models**: Define relationships, scopes, accessors, `$fillable`, `casts()` method
1604: 
1605: ### Frontend
1606: - **Import alias**: `@/` for [resources/js/](resources/js/) (configured in [jsconfig.json](jsconfig.json))
1607: - **Layouts**: [AuthenticatedLayout](resources/js/Layouts/AuthenticatedLayout.jsx) for auth pages, `GuestLayout` for public
1608: - **Components**: Reusable, located in [Components/](resources/js/Components), forward refs, spread props
1609: - **Pages**: Default export, wrap with layouts, use `<Head title="...">`, use `useForm()` for forms
1610: - **Page Structure**: Modular composition (Next.js pattern) - keep pages thin (~50-100 lines), extract sections to `Partials/` for page-specific components or `Components/` for reusable ones
1611: - **Routing**: ALWAYS use `route('name')` helper (Ziggy), never hardcode URLs
1612: - **Forms**: Use Inertia's `useForm()` hook, show `processing` state, display `errors`
1613: 
1614: ### Testing
1615: - Feature tests: Test user flows, interactions, full request cycle ([ProfileTest.php](tests/Feature/ProfileTest.php))
1616: - Unit tests: Test business logic in isolation (Services, Actions, Repositories)
1617: - Use `RefreshDatabase` trait, factories, descriptive test names with `test_` prefix
1618: - Assert fluently: `assertOk()`, `assertRedirect()`, `assertSessionHasNoErrors()`, `assertDatabaseHas()`
1619: 
1620: ---
1621: 
1622: ## Integration Points
1623: 
1624: - **Database sessions**: [config/session.php](config/session.php), uses `sessions` table
1625: - **Email verification**: User model supports `MustVerifyEmail` contract (implement if needed)
1626: - **Queue**: Configured for database driver, `jobs` table exists, use for long-running tasks
1627: - **CSRF**: Auto-protected via [bootstrap.js](resources/js/bootstrap.js) - axios sends `X-CSRF-TOKEN`
1628: - **Rate limiting**: Applied in [routes/auth.php](routes/auth.php), use for sensitive endpoints
1629: 
1630: ---
1631: 
1632: ## Never Do (Anti-Patterns)
1633: 
1634: ### Backend
1635: - ✗ Business logic in controllers (extract to Service/Action)
1636: - ✗ Return JSON from web routes (use `Inertia::render()` only)
1637: - ✗ Direct Eloquent calls for complex queries (use Repository)
1638: - ✗ Authorization in controllers (use Policy)
1639: - ✗ Inline validation for complex forms (use FormRequest)
1640: - ✗ Raw SQL with user input (always use parameter binding)
1641: - ✗ Models without `$fillable` or mass assignment protection
1642: - ✗ Expose sensitive data in responses (use `$hidden` in models)
1643: - ✗ Skip transactions for multi-step operations
1644: 
1645: ### Frontend
1646: - ✗ Hardcode URLs (use `route()` helper)
1647: - ✗ Duplicate components (extract to [Components/](resources/js/Components))
1648: - ✗ Skip error display (show `errors` from `useForm()`)
1649: - ✗ Ignore `processing` state (disable buttons during submission)
1650: - ✗ Direct API calls (Inertia handles data flow)
1651: - ✗ Monolithic pages (pages > 100 lines should be modularized into Partials)
1652: 
1653: ### Security
1654: - ✗ Commit `.env` file
1655: - ✗ Trust user input without validation
1656: - ✗ Skip authorization checks
1657: - ✗ Disable CSRF protection
1658: - ✗ Use `APP_DEBUG=true` in production
1659: - ✗ Store passwords in plain text
1660: - ✗ Expose internal error details to users
1661: 
1662: ### Testing
1663: - ✗ Skip tests for new features (ALWAYS write tests)
1664: - ✗ Test implementation details (test behavior, not internals)
1665: - ✗ Ignore test failures (fix immediately)
1666: - ✗ Write tests without assertions
1667: - ✗ Commit code without running test suite
1668: - ✗ Mock everything (test real interactions when possible)
1669: 
1670: ---
1671: 
1672: ## Summary: Core Principles
1673: 
1674: 1. **Test-First Development**: ALWAYS write tests for new features (TDD approach preferred)
1675: 2. **Separation of Concerns**: Controllers delegate, Services orchestrate, Actions focus, Repositories abstract
1676: 3. **DRY Principle**: Extract shared logic, reusable components, no duplication
1677: 4. **Security First**: Validate everything, authorize always, protect headers, sanitize input
1678: 5. **Testable**: 70%+ coverage, isolated unit tests, comprehensive feature tests, all tests pass before commit
1679: 6. **Scalable**: Caching, queues, optimized queries, event-driven when needed
1680: 7. **Maintainable**: Clear naming, organized structure, documented decisions
1681: 8. **Modular Pages**: Keep pages thin (~50-100 lines), extract sections to Partials/Components (Next.js pattern)
1682: 9. **Type Safety**: Move toward TypeScript, use JSDoc, validate props
1683: 10. **User Experience**: Loading states, error boundaries, friendly messages
1684: 
1685: **For AI Agents**: 
1686: - **ALWAYS suggest writing tests** when implementing features - this is non-negotiable for professional development
1687: - **Suggest modularization** when pages exceed 100 lines - extract to Partials or Components
1688: - Present architectural options with pros/cons for major decisions
1689: - Follow these standards strictly
1690: - When in doubt, ask the user rather than assuming
1691: - Before marking work complete, ensure tests are written and passing
1692: 
1693: 
1694: ## vexp context tools <!-- vexp v1.2.30 -->
1695: 
1696: **MANDATORY: use `run_pipeline` — do NOT grep, glob, or read files manually.**
1697: vexp returns pre-indexed, graph-ranked context in a single call.
1698: 
1699: ### Workflow
1700: 1. `run_pipeline` with your task description — ALWAYS FIRST (replaces all other tools)
1701: 2. Make targeted changes based on the context returned
1702: 3. `run_pipeline` again only if you need more context
1703: 
1704: ### Available MCP tools
1705: - `run_pipeline` — **PRIMARY TOOL**. Runs capsule + impact + memory in 1 call.
1706:   Auto-detects intent. Includes file content. Example: `run_pipeline({ "task": "fix auth bug" })`
1707: - `get_context_capsule` — lightweight, for simple questions only
1708: - `get_impact_graph` — impact analysis of a specific symbol
1709: - `search_logic_flow` — execution paths between functions
1710: - `get_skeleton` — compact file structure
1711: - `index_status` — indexing status
1712: - `get_session_context` — recall observations from sessions
1713: - `search_memory` — cross-session search
1714: - `save_observation` — persist insights (prefer run_pipeline's observation param)
1715: 
1716: ### Agentic search
1717: - Do NOT use built-in file search, grep, or codebase indexing — always call `run_pipeline` first
1718: - If you spawn sub-agents or background tasks, pass them the context from `run_pipeline`
1719:   rather than letting them search the codebase independently
1720: 
1721: ### Smart Features
1722: Intent auto-detection, hybrid ranking, session memory, auto-expanding budget.
1723: 
1724: ### Multi-Repo
1725: `run_pipeline` auto-queries all indexed repos. Use `repos: ["alias"]` to scope. Run `index_status` to see aliases.
1726: <!-- /vexp -->

================
File: app/Actions/Admin/BulkDestroyProcessedDocuments.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: 
 3: namespace App\Actions\Admin;
 4: 
 5: use App\Models\DataRequest;
 6: use App\Models\RequestDocument;
 7: use App\Enums\RequestStatus;
 8: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;
 9: 
10: class BulkDestroyProcessedDocuments
11: {
12:     public function handle(DataRequest $dataRequest, array $documentIds): int
13:     {
14:         $documents = RequestDocument::query()
15:             ->where('data_request_id', $dataRequest->id)
16:             ->where('document_type', 'processed_data_result')
17:             ->whereIn('id', $documentIds)
18:             ->get();
19: 
20:         abort_if($documents->count() !== count($documentIds), 404, 'Sebagian dokumen hasil tidak ditemukan.');
21: 
22:         foreach ($documents as $document) {
23:             if ($document->file_path) {
24:                 Storage::delete($document->file_path);
25:             }
26:             $document->delete();
27:         }
28: 
29:         $remainingCount = RequestDocument::query()
30:             ->where('data_request_id', $dataRequest->id)
31:             ->where('document_type', 'processed_data_result')
32:             ->count();
33: 
34:         if ($remainingCount === 0 && $dataRequest->status === RequestStatus::Completed) {
35:             $dataRequest->update(['status' => RequestStatus::Processing]);
36:         }
37: 
38:         activity()
39:             ->performedOn($dataRequest)
40:             ->causedBy(auth()->user())
41:             ->withProperties([
42:                 'deleted_count' => $documents->count(),
43:             ])
44:             ->event('processed_documents_bulk_deleted')
45:             ->log('Admin menghapus beberapa dokumen hasil sekaligus.');
46: 
47:         return $documents->count();
48:     }
49: }
