 79:     */
 80: 
 81:     'locale' => env('APP_LOCALE', 'id'),
 82: 
 83:     'fallback_locale' => env('APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE', 'id'),
 84: 
 85:     'faker_locale' => env('APP_FAKER_LOCALE', 'en_US'),
 86: 
 87:     /*
 88:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 89:     | Encryption Key
 90:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 91:     |
 92:     | This key is utilized by Laravel's encryption services and should be set
 93:     | to a random, 32 character string to ensure that all encrypted values
 94:     | are secure. You should do this prior to deploying the application.
 95:     |
 96:     */
 97: 
 98:     'cipher' => 'AES-256-CBC',
 99: 
100:     'key' => env('APP_KEY'),
101: 
102:     'previous_keys' => [
103:         ...array_filter(
104:             explode(',', (string) env('APP_PREVIOUS_KEYS', ''))
105:         ),
106:     ],
107: 
108:     /*
109:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
110:     | Maintenance Mode Driver
111:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
112:     |
113:     | These configuration options determine the driver used to determine and
114:     | manage Laravel's "maintenance mode" status. The "cache" driver will
115:     | allow maintenance mode to be controlled across multiple machines.
116:     |
117:     | Supported drivers: "file", "cache"
118:     |
119:     */
120: 
121:     'maintenance' => [
122:         'driver' => env('APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER', 'file'),
123:         'store' => env('APP_MAINTENANCE_STORE', 'database'),
124:     ],
125: 
126: ];

================
File: config/auth.php
================
  1: <?php
  2: 
  3: return [
  4: 
  5:     /*
  6:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  7:     | Authentication Defaults
  8:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  9:     |
 10:     | This option defines the default authentication "guard" and password
 11:     | reset "broker" for your application. You may change these values
 12:     | as required, but they're a perfect start for most applications.
 13:     |
 14:     */
 15: 
 16:     'defaults' => [
 17:         'guard' => env('AUTH_GUARD', 'web'),
 18:         'passwords' => env('AUTH_PASSWORD_BROKER', 'users'),
 19:     ],
 20: 
 21:     /*
 22:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 23:     | Authentication Guards
 24:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 25:     |
 26:     | Next, you may define every authentication guard for your application.
 27:     | Of course, a great default configuration has been defined for you
 28:     | which utilizes session storage plus the Eloquent user provider.
 29:     |
 30:     | All authentication guards have a user provider, which defines how the
 31:     | users are actually retrieved out of your database or other storage
 32:     | system used by the application. Typically, Eloquent is utilized.
 33:     |
 34:     | Supported: "session"
 35:     |
 36:     */
 37: 
 38:     'guards' => [
 39:         'web' => [
 40:             'driver' => 'session',
 41:             'provider' => 'users',
 42:         ],
 43:     ],
 44: 
 45:     /*
 46:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 47:     | User Providers
 48:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 49:     |
 50:     | All authentication guards have a user provider, which defines how the
 51:     | users are actually retrieved out of your database or other storage
 52:     | system used by the application. Typically, Eloquent is utilized.
 53:     |
 54:     | If you have multiple user tables or models you may configure multiple
 55:     | providers to represent the model / table. These providers may then
 56:     | be assigned to any extra authentication guards you have defined.
 57:     |
 58:     | Supported: "database", "eloquent"
 59:     |
 60:     */
 61: 
 62:     'providers' => [
 63:         'users' => [
 64:             'driver' => 'eloquent',
 65:             'model' => env('AUTH_MODEL', App\Models\User::class),
 66:         ],
 67: 
 68:         // 'users' => [
 69:         //     'driver' => 'database',
 70:         //     'table' => 'users',
 71:         // ],
 72:     ],
 73: 
 74:     /*
 75:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 76:     | Resetting Passwords
 77:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 78:     |
 79:     | These configuration options specify the behavior of Laravel's password
 80:     | reset functionality, including the table utilized for token storage
 81:     | and the user provider that is invoked to actually retrieve users.
 82:     |
 83:     | The expiry time is the number of minutes that each reset token will be
 84:     | considered valid. This security feature keeps tokens short-lived so
 85:     | they have less time to be guessed. You may change this as needed.
 86:     |
 87:     | The throttle setting is the number of seconds a user must wait before
 88:     | generating more password reset tokens. This prevents the user from
 89:     | quickly generating a very large amount of password reset tokens.
 90:     |
 91:     */
 92: 
 93:     'passwords' => [
 94:         'users' => [
 95:             'provider' => 'users',
 96:             'table' => env('AUTH_PASSWORD_RESET_TOKEN_TABLE', 'password_reset_tokens'),
 97:             'expire' => 60,
 98:             'throttle' => 60,
 99:         ],
100:     ],
101: 
102:     /*
103:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
104:     | Password Confirmation Timeout
105:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
106:     |
107:     | Here you may define the number of seconds before a password confirmation
108:     | window expires and users are asked to re-enter their password via the
109:     | confirmation screen. By default, the timeout lasts for three hours.
110:     |
111:     */
112: 
113:     'password_timeout' => env('AUTH_PASSWORD_TIMEOUT', 10800),
114: 
115: ];

  1: <?php
  2: 
  3: use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  4: 
  5: return [
  6: 
  7:     /*
  8:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  9:     | Default Cache Store
 10:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------

 14:     | specified when running a cache operation inside the application.
 15:     |
 16:     */
 17: 
 18:     'default' => env('CACHE_STORE', 'database'),
 19: 
 20:     /*
 21:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 22:     | Cache Stores
 23:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 24:     |
 25:     | Here you may define all of the cache "stores" for your application as
 26:     | well as their drivers. You may even define multiple stores for the
 27:     | same cache driver to group types of items stored in your caches.
 28:     |
 29:     | Supported drivers: "array", "database", "file", "memcached",
 30:     |                    "redis", "dynamodb", "octane",
 31:     |                    "failover", "null"
 32:     |
 33:     */
 34: 
 35:     'stores' => [
 36: 
 37:         'array' => [
 38:             'driver' => 'array',
 39:             'serialize' => false,
 40:         ],
 41: 
 42:         'database' => [
 43:             'driver' => 'database',
 44:             'connection' => env('DB_CACHE_CONNECTION'),
 45:             'table' => env('DB_CACHE_TABLE', 'cache'),
 46:             'lock_connection' => env('DB_CACHE_LOCK_CONNECTION'),
 47:             'lock_table' => env('DB_CACHE_LOCK_TABLE'),
 48:         ],
 49: 
 50:         'file' => [
 51:             'driver' => 'file',
 52:             'path' => storage_path('framework/cache/data'),
 53:             'lock_path' => storage_path('framework/cache/data'),
 54:         ],
 55: 
 56:         'memcached' => [
 57:             'driver' => 'memcached',
 58:             'persistent_id' => env('MEMCACHED_PERSISTENT_ID'),
 59:             'sasl' => [
 60:                 env('MEMCACHED_USERNAME'),
 61:                 env('MEMCACHED_PASSWORD'),
 62:             ],
 63:             'options' => [
 64:                 // Memcached::OPT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT => 2000,
 65:             ],
 66:             'servers' => [
 67:                 [
 68:                     'host' => env('MEMCACHED_HOST', '127.0.0.1'),
 69:                     'port' => env('MEMCACHED_PORT', 11211),
 70:                     'weight' => 100,
 71:                 ],
 72:             ],
 73:         ],
 74: 
 75:         'redis' => [
 76:             'driver' => 'redis',
 77:             'connection' => env('REDIS_CACHE_CONNECTION', 'cache'),
 78:             'lock_connection' => env('REDIS_CACHE_LOCK_CONNECTION', 'default'),
 79:         ],
 80: 
 81:         'dynamodb' => [
 82:             'driver' => 'dynamodb',
 83:             'key' => env('AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID'),
 84:             'secret' => env('AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY'),
 85:             'region' => env('AWS_DEFAULT_REGION', 'us-east-1'),
 86:             'table' => env('DYNAMODB_CACHE_TABLE', 'cache'),
 87:             'endpoint' => env('DYNAMODB_ENDPOINT'),
 88:         ],
 89: 
 90:         'octane' => [
 91:             'driver' => 'octane',
 92:         ],
 93: 
 94:         'failover' => [
 95:             'driver' => 'failover',
 96:             'stores' => [
 97:                 'database',
 98:                 'array',
 99:             ],
100:         ],
101: 
102:     ],
103: 
104:     /*
105:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
106:     | Cache Key Prefix
107:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
108:     |
109:     | When utilizing the APC, database, memcached, Redis, and DynamoDB cache
110:     | stores, there might be other applications using the same cache. For
111:     | that reason, you may prefix every cache key to avoid collisions.
112:     |
113:     */
114: 
115:     'prefix' => env('CACHE_PREFIX', Str::slug((string) env('APP_NAME', 'laravel')).'-cache-'),
116: 
117: ];

File: config/logging.php

  2: 
  3: use Monolog\Handler\NullHandler;
  4: use Monolog\Handler\StreamHandler;
  5: use Monolog\Handler\SyslogUdpHandler;
  6: use Monolog\Processor\PsrLogMessageProcessor;
  7: 
  8: return [
  9: 
 10:     /*
 11:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 12:     | Default Log Channel
 13:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 14:     |
 15:     | This option defines the default log channel that is utilized to write
 16:     | messages to your logs. The value provided here should match one of
 17:     | the channels present in the list of "channels" configured below.
 18:     |
 19:     */
 20: 
 21:     'default' => env('LOG_CHANNEL', 'stack'),
 22: 
 23:     /*
 24:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 25:     | Deprecations Log Channel
 26:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 27:     |
 28:     | This option controls the log channel that should be used to log warnings
 29:     | regarding deprecated PHP and library features. This allows you to get
 30:     | your application ready for upcoming major versions of dependencies.
 31:     |
 32:     */
 33: 
 34:     'deprecations' => [
 35:         'channel' => env('LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL', 'null'),
 36:         'trace' => env('LOG_DEPRECATIONS_TRACE', false),
 37:     ],
 38: 
 39:     /*
 40:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 41:     | Log Channels
 42:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 43:     |
 44:     | Here you may configure the log channels for your application. Laravel
 45:     | utilizes the Monolog PHP logging library, which includes a variety
 46:     | of powerful log handlers and formatters that you're free to use.
 47:     |
 48:     | Available drivers: "single", "daily", "slack", "syslog",
 49:     |                    "errorlog", "monolog", "custom", "stack"
 50:     |
 51:     */
 52: 
 53:     'channels' => [
 54: 
 55:         'stack' => [
 56:             'driver' => 'stack',
 57:             'channels' => explode(',', (string) env('LOG_STACK', 'single')),
 58:             'ignore_exceptions' => false,
 59:         ],
 60: 
 61:         'single' => [
 62:             'driver' => 'single',
 63:             'path' => storage_path('logs/laravel.log'),
 64:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
 65:             'replace_placeholders' => true,
 66:         ],
 67: 
 68:         'daily' => [
 69:             'driver' => 'daily',
 70:             'path' => storage_path('logs/laravel.log'),
 71:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
 72:             'days' => env('LOG_DAILY_DAYS', 14),
 73:             'replace_placeholders' => true,
 74:         ],
 75: 
 76:         'slack' => [
 77:             'driver' => 'slack',
 78:             'url' => env('LOG_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL'),
 79:             'username' => env('LOG_SLACK_USERNAME', 'Laravel Log'),
 80:             'emoji' => env('LOG_SLACK_EMOJI', ':boom:'),
 81:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'critical'),
 82:             'replace_placeholders' => true,
 83:         ],
 84: 
 85:         'papertrail' => [
 86:             'driver' => 'monolog',
 87:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
 88:             'handler' => env('LOG_PAPERTRAIL_HANDLER', SyslogUdpHandler::class),
 89:             'handler_with' => [
 90:                 'host' => env('PAPERTRAIL_URL'),
 91:                 'port' => env('PAPERTRAIL_PORT'),
 92:                 'connectionString' => 'tls://'.env('PAPERTRAIL_URL').':'.env('PAPERTRAIL_PORT'),
 93:             ],
 94:             'processors' => [PsrLogMessageProcessor::class],
 95:         ],
 96: 
 97:         'stderr' => [
 98:             'driver' => 'monolog',
 99:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
100:             'handler' => StreamHandler::class,
101:             'handler_with' => [
102:                 'stream' => 'php://stderr',
103:             ],
104:             'formatter' => env('LOG_STDERR_FORMATTER'),
105:             'processors' => [PsrLogMessageProcessor::class],
106:         ],
107: 
108:         'syslog' => [
109:             'driver' => 'syslog',
110:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
111:             'facility' => env('LOG_SYSLOG_FACILITY', LOG_USER),
112:             'replace_placeholders' => true,
113:         ],
114:

116:             'driver' => 'errorlog',
117:             'level' => env('LOG_LEVEL', 'debug'),
118:             'replace_placeholders' => true,
119:         ],
120:

122:             'driver' => 'monolog',
123:             'handler' => NullHandler::class,
124:         ],

128:         ],

 26:     |

================
File: config/session.php
================
  1: <?php
  2: 
  3: use Illuminate\Support\Str;
  4: 
  5: return [
  6: 
  7:     /*
  8:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
  9:     | Default Session Driver
 10:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 11:     |
 12:     | This option determines the default session driver that is utilized for
 13:     | incoming requests. Laravel supports a variety of storage options to
 14:     | persist session data. Database storage is a great default choice.
 15:     |
 16:     | Supported: "file", "cookie", "database", "memcached",
 17:     |            "redis", "dynamodb", "array"
 18:     |
 19:     */
 20: 
 21:     'driver' => env('SESSION_DRIVER', 'database'),
 22: 
 23:     /*
 24:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 25:     | Session Lifetime
 26:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 27:     |
 28:     | Here you may specify the number of minutes that you wish the session
 29:     | to be allowed to remain idle before it expires. If you want them
 30:     | to expire immediately when the browser is closed then you may
 31:     | indicate that via the expire_on_close configuration option.
 32:     |
 33:     */
 34: 
 35:     'lifetime' => (int) env('SESSION_LIFETIME', 120),
 36: 
 37:     'expire_on_close' => env('SESSION_EXPIRE_ON_CLOSE', false),
 38: 
 39:     /*
 40:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 41:     | Session Encryption
 42:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 43:     |
 44:     | This option allows you to easily specify that all of your session data
 45:     | should be encrypted before it's stored. All encryption is performed
 46:     | automatically by Laravel and you may use the session like normal.
 47:     |
 48:     */
 49: 
 50:     'encrypt' => env('SESSION_ENCRYPT', false),
 51: 
 52:     /*
 53:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 54:     | Session File Location
 55:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 56:     |
 57:     | When utilizing the "file" session driver, the session files are placed
 58:     | on disk. The default storage location is defined here; however, you
 59:     | are free to provide another location where they should be stored.
 60:     |
 61:     */
 62: 
 63:     'files' => storage_path('framework/sessions'),
 64: 
 65:     /*
 66:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 67:     | Session Database Connection
 68:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 69:     |
 70:     | When using the "database" or "redis" session drivers, you may specify a
 71:     | connection that should be used to manage these sessions. This should
 72:     | correspond to a connection in your database configuration options.
 73:     |
 74:     */
 75: 
 76:     'connection' => env('SESSION_CONNECTION'),
 77: 
 78:     /*
 79:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 80:     | Session Database Table
 81:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 82:     |
 83:     | When using the "database" session driver, you may specify the table to
 84:     | be used to store sessions. Of course, a sensible default is defined
 85:     | for you; however, you're welcome to change this to another table.
 86:     |
 87:     */
 88: 
 89:     'table' => env('SESSION_TABLE', 'sessions'),
 90: 
 91:     /*
 92:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 93:     | Session Cache Store
 94:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
 95:     |
 96:     | When using one of the framework's cache driven session backends, you may
 97:     | define the cache store which should be used to store the session data
 98:     | between requests. This must match one of your defined cache stores.
 99:     |
100:     | Affects: "dynamodb", "memcached", "redis"
101:     |
102:     */
103: 
104:     'store' => env('SESSION_STORE'),
105: 
106:     /*
107:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
108:     | Session Sweeping Lottery
109:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
110:     |
111:     | Some session drivers must manually sweep their storage location to get
112:     | rid of old sessions from storage. Here are the chances that it will
113:     | happen on a given request. By default, the odds are 2 out of 100.
114:     |
115:     */
116: 
117:     'lottery' => [2, 100],
118: 
119:     /*
120:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
121:     | Session Cookie Name
122:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
123:     |
124:     | Here you may change the name of the session cookie that is created by
125:     | the framework. Typically, you should not need to change this value
126:     | since doing so does not grant a meaningful security improvement.
127:     |
128:     */
129: 
130:     'cookie' => env(
131:         'SESSION_COOKIE',
132:         Str::slug((string) env('APP_NAME', 'laravel')).'-session'
133:     ),
134: 
135:     /*
136:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
137:     | Session Cookie Path
138:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
139:     |
140:     | The session cookie path determines the path for which the cookie will
141:     | be regarded as available. Typically, this will be the root path of
142:     | your application, but you're free to change this when necessary.
143:     |
144:     */
145: 
146:     'path' => env('SESSION_PATH', '/'),
147: 
148:     /*
149:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
150:     | Session Cookie Domain
151:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
152:     |
153:     | This value determines the domain and subdomains the session cookie is
154:     | available to. By default, the cookie will be available to the root
155:     | domain without subdomains. Typically, this shouldn't be changed.
156:     |
157:     */
158: 
159:     'domain' => env('SESSION_DOMAIN'),
160: 
161:     /*
162:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
163:     | HTTPS Only Cookies
164:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
165:     |
166:     | By setting this option to true, session cookies will only be sent back
167:     | to the server if the browser has a HTTPS connection. This will keep
168:     | the cookie from being sent to you when it can't be done securely.
169:     |
170:     */
171: 
172:     'secure' => env('SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE'),
173: 
174:     /*
175:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
176:     | HTTP Access Only
177:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
178:     |
179:     | Setting this value to true will prevent JavaScript from accessing the
180:     | value of the cookie and the cookie will only be accessible through
181:     | the HTTP protocol. It's unlikely you should disable this option.
182:     |
183:     */
184: 
185:     'http_only' => env('SESSION_HTTP_ONLY', true),
186: 
187:     /*
188:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
189:     | Same-Site Cookies
190:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
191:     |
192:     | This option determines how your cookies behave when cross-site requests
193:     | take place, and can be used to mitigate CSRF attacks. By default, we
194:     | will set this value to "lax" to permit secure cross-site requests.
195:     |
196:     | See: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie#samesitesamesite-value
197:     |
198:     | Supported: "lax", "strict", "none", null
199:     |
200:     */
201: 
202:     'same_site' => env('SESSION_SAME_SITE', 'lax'),
203: 
204:     /*
205:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
206:     | Partitioned Cookies
207:     |--------------------------------------------------------------------------
208:     |
209:     | Setting this value to true will tie the cookie to the top-level site for
210:     | a cross-site context. Partitioned cookies are accepted by the browser
211:     | when flagged "secure" and the Same-Site attribute is set to "none".
212:     |
213:     */
214: 
215:     'partitioned' => env('SESSION_PARTITIONED_COOKIE', false),
216: 
217: ];

================
File: database/factories/DataRequestFactory.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: 
 3: namespace Database\Factories;
 4: 
 5: use App\Enums\PurposeType;
 6: use App\Enums\RequestCategory;
 7: use App\Enums\RequestStatus;
 8: use App\Models\DataRequest;
 9: use App\Models\User;
10: use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory;
11: 
12: /**
13:  * @extends \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory<\App\Models\DataRequest>
14:  */
15: class DataRequestFactory extends Factory
16: {
17:     protected $model = DataRequest::class;
18: 
19:     public function definition(): array
20:     {
21:         return [
22:             'request_code' => DataRequest::generateRequestCode(),
23:             'title' => fake()->sentence(3),
24:             'user_id' => User::factory(),
25:             'purpose_type' => fake()->randomElement(PurposeType::cases()),
26:             'request_category' => fake()->randomElement(RequestCategory::cases()),
27:             'status' => RequestStatus::Pending,
28:         ];
29:     }
30: 
31:     /**
32:      * Set the request as approved.
33:      */
34:     public function approved(): static
35:     {
36:         return $this->state(fn(array $attributes) => [
37:             'status' => RequestStatus::Approved,
38:             'reviewed_by' => User::factory()->create(['role' => \App\Enums\UserRole::Admin])->id,
39:             'reviewed_at' => now(),
40:         ]);
41:     }
42: 
43:     /**
44:      * Set the request as rejected.
45:      */
46:     public function rejected(): static
47:     {
48:         return $this->state(fn(array $attributes) => [
49:             'status' => RequestStatus::Rejected,
50:             'rejection_reason' => fake()->sentence(),
51:             'reviewed_by' => User::factory()->create(['role' => \App\Enums\UserRole::Admin])->id,
52:             'reviewed_at' => now(),
53:         ]);
54:     }
55: }

================
File: database/factories/FaqFactory.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: 
 3: namespace Database\Factories;
 4: 
 5: use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory;

File: database/factories/SurveyFactory.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: 
 3: namespace Database\Factories;
 4: 
 5: use App\Models\DataRequest;
 6: use App\Enums\SurveyStatus;
 7: use App\Enums\SurveyType;
 8: use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory;
 9: 
10: class SurveyFactory extends Factory
11: {
12:     public function definition(): array
13:     {
14:         return [
15:             'data_request_id' => DataRequest::factory(),
16:             'survey_type' => $this->faker->randomElement([SurveyType::Ekm, SurveyType::Ikm]),
17:             'proof_path' => 'survey_proofs/' . $this->faker->uuid() . '.jpg',
18:             'proof_original_name' => 'survey_proof.jpg',
19:             'status' => SurveyStatus::Pending,
20:         ];
21:     }
22: 
23:     public function approved(): static
24:     {
25:         return $this->state(fn (array $attributes) => [
26:             'status' => SurveyStatus::Approved,
27:             'reviewed_by' => \App\Models\User::factory()->admin(),
28:             'reviewed_at' => now(),
29:         ]);
30:     }
31: 
32:     public function rejected(): static
33:     {
34:         return $this->state(fn (array $attributes) => [
35:             'status' => SurveyStatus::Rejected,
36:             'rejection_reason' => 'Gambar buram',
37:             'reviewed_by' => \App\Models\User::factory()->admin(),
38:             'reviewed_at' => now(),
39:         ]);
40:     }
41: }

File: database/factories/UserFactory.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: 
 3: namespace Database\Factories;
 4: 
 5: use App\Enums\UserRole;
 6: use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory;
 7: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash;
 8: use Illuminate\Support\Str;
 9: 
10: /**
11:  * @extends \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory<\App\Models\User>
12:  */
13: class UserFactory extends Factory
14: {
15:     /**
16:      * The current password being used by the factory.
17:      */
18:     protected static ?string $password;
19: 
20:     /**
21:      * Define the model's default state.
22:      *
23:      * @return array<string, mixed>
24:      */
25:     public function definition(): array
26:     {
27:         return [
28:             'name' => fake()->name(),
29:             'email' => fake()->unique()->safeEmail(),
30:             'phone' => fake()->numerify('08##########'),
31:             'company_name' => fake()->optional(0.5)->company(),
32:             'role' => UserRole::User,
33:             'email_verified_at' => now(),
34:             'password' => static::$password ??= Hash::make('password'),
35:             'remember_token' => Str::random(10),
36:         ];
37:     }
38: 
39:     /**
40:      * Indicate that the model's email address should be unverified.
41:      */
42:     public function unverified(): static
43:     {
44:         return $this->state(fn(array $attributes) => [
45:             'email_verified_at' => null,
46:         ]);
47:     }
48: 
49:     /**
50:      * Indicate that the model's role should be Admin.
51:      */
52:     public function admin(): static
53:     {
54:         return $this->state(fn(array $attributes) => [
55:             'role' => \App\Enums\UserRole::Admin,
56:         ]);
57:     }
58: }

File: database/migrations/2026_05_10_153000_change_catalog_items_price_to_unsigned_bigint.php
================
  1: <?php
  2: 
  3: use Illuminate\Database\Migrations\Migration;
  4: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
  5: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Schema;
  6: 
  7: return new class extends Migration
  8: {
  9:     public function up(): void
 10:     {
 11:         if (!Schema::hasColumn('catalog_items', 'price')) {
 12:             return;
 13:         }
 14: 
 15:         $driver = DB::getDriverName();
 16: 
 17:         if ($driver === 'mysql') {
 18:             // Keep digits only so values like "200.000" or "Rp 200000" become numeric.
 19:             DB::statement("
 20:                 UPDATE catalog_items
 21:                 SET price = REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(price, ''), '[^0-9]', '')
 22:             ");
 23: 
 24:             // Empty values become zero before type conversion.
 25:             DB::statement("
 26:                 UPDATE catalog_items
 27:                 SET price = '0'
 28:                 WHERE price IS NULL OR price = ''
 29:             ");
 30: 
 31:             DB::statement("
 32:                 ALTER TABLE catalog_items
 33:                 MODIFY price BIGINT UNSIGNED NOT NULL
 34:             ");
 35: 
 36:             return;
 37:         }
 38: 
 39:         if ($driver === 'pgsql') {
 40:             DB::statement("
 41:                 UPDATE catalog_items
 42:                 SET price = REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(price::text, ''), '[^0-9]', '', 'g')
 43:             ");
 44: 
 45:             DB::statement("
 46:                 UPDATE catalog_items
 47:                 SET price = '0'
 48:                 WHERE price IS NULL OR price = ''
 49:             ");
 50: 
 51:             DB::statement("
 52:                 ALTER TABLE catalog_items
 53:                 ALTER COLUMN price TYPE BIGINT
 54:                 USING price::bigint
 55:             ");
 56: 
 57:             DB::statement("
 58:                 ALTER TABLE catalog_items
 59:                 ALTER COLUMN price SET NOT NULL
 60:             ");
 61: 
 62:             return;
 63:         }
 64: 
 65:         // Add support for SQLite in testing environment: skip migration.
 66:         if ($driver === 'sqlite') {
 67:             // SQLite does not support altering column types in the same way; skipping is acceptable for tests.
 68:             return;
 69:         }
 70:         throw new RuntimeException('Unsupported database driver for price type migration: ' . $driver);
 71:     }
 72: 
 73:     public function down(): void
 74:     {
 75:         if (!Schema::hasColumn('catalog_items', 'price')) {
 76:             return;
 77:         }
 78: 
 79:         $driver = DB::getDriverName();
 80: 
 81:         if ($driver === 'mysql') {
 82:             DB::statement("
 83:                 ALTER TABLE catalog_items
 84:                 MODIFY price VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
 85:             ");
 86: 
 87:             return;
 88:         }
 89: 
 90:         if ($driver === 'pgsql') {
 91:             DB::statement("
 92:                 ALTER TABLE catalog_items
 93:                 ALTER COLUMN price TYPE VARCHAR(255)
 94:                 USING price::text
 95:             ");
 96: 
 97:             return;
 98:         }
 99: 
100:         if ($driver === 'sqlite') {
101:             // SQLite rollback not needed as migration was skipped.
102:             return;
103:         }
104:         throw new RuntimeException('Unsupported database driver for price rollback: ' . $driver);
105:     }
106: };

================
File: database/seeders/DataRequestSeeder.php
================
  1: <?php
  2: 
  3: namespace Database\Seeders;
  4: 
  5: use App\Enums\PurposeType;
  6: use App\Enums\RequestCategory;
  7: use App\Enums\RequestStatus;
  8: use App\Enums\UserRole;
  9: use App\Models\DataRequest;
 10: use App\Models\RejectionTemplate;
 11: use App\Models\RequestDocument;
 12: use App\Models\User;
 13: use Illuminate\Database\Seeder;
 14: use Illuminate\Http\File;
 15: use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;
 16: 
 17: class DataRequestSeeder extends Seeder
 18: {
 19:     /**
 20:     * Seed data requests for paid and free categories with their correct flows.
 21:      *
 22:      * Creates:
 23:      * - 1 regular user account
 24:     * - Commercial (paid): includes payment flow statuses
 25:     * - Non-commercial (free): skips payment flow and goes directly to processing
 26:      */
 27:     public function run(): void
 28:     {
 29:         // Ensure rejection templates exist first
 30:         $this->call(RejectionTemplateSeeder::class);
 31: 
 32:         // Get the admin for reviewing
 33:         $admin = User::where('role', UserRole::Admin)->first();
 34: 
 35:         // Create a regular user account
 36:         $user = User::firstOrCreate(
 37:             ['email' => 'user@example.com'],
 38:             [
 39:                 'name' => 'Pengguna Demo',
 40:                 'phone' => '+6281234567890',
 41:                 'role' => UserRole::User,
 42:                 'email_verified_at' => now(),
 43:                 'password' => bcrypt('password'),
 44:             ]
 45:         );
 46: 
 47:         // Remove previously seeded demo requests so rerunning the seeder does not
 48:         // leave legacy payment-flow data behind.
 49:         DataRequest::withTrashed()
 50:             ->where('user_id', $user->id)
 51:             ->get()
 52:             ->each
 53:             ->forceDelete();
 54: 
 55:         // Source PDFs from database/seeders/data/
 56:         $sourcePdfs = [
 57:             database_path('seeders/data/Dokumen 1.pdf'),
 58:             database_path('seeders/data/Dokumen 2.pdf'),
 59:             database_path('seeders/data/Dokumen 3.pdf'),
 60:             database_path('seeders/data/Dokumen 4.pdf'),
 61:         ];
 62: 
 63:         $rejectionTemplates = RejectionTemplate::all();
 64:         $purposeTypes = PurposeType::cases();
 65: 
 66:         // ── 1. Pending (per category) ─────────────────────────────
 67:         foreach ($purposeTypes as $purposeType) {
 68:             $this->createRequest(
 69:                 user: $user,
 70:                 purposeType: $purposeType,
 71:                 status: RequestStatus::Pending,
 72:                 reviewer: null,
 73:                 rejectionReason: null,
 74:                 sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
 75:                 titleSuffix: 'Menunggu Review',
 76:             );
 77: 
 78:             foreach ($rejectionTemplates as $template) {
 79:                 $this->createRequest(
 80:                     user: $user,
 81:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
 82:                     status: RequestStatus::Rejected,
 83:                     reviewer: $admin,
 84:                     rejectionReason: $template->body,
 85:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
 86:                     titleSuffix: "Ditolak - {$template->title}",
 87:                 );
 88:             }
 89: 
 90:             if ($purposeType->category() === RequestCategory::Paid) {
 91:                 $this->createRequest(
 92:                     user: $user,
 93:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
 94:                     status: RequestStatus::PaymentRequired,
 95:                     reviewer: $admin,
 96:                     rejectionReason: null,
 97:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
 98:                     titleSuffix: 'Menunggu Pembayaran',
 99:                 );
100: 
101:                 $this->createRequest(
102:                     user: $user,
103:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
104:                     status: RequestStatus::PaymentVerifying,
105:                     reviewer: $admin,
106:                     rejectionReason: null,
107:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
108:                     titleSuffix: 'Verifikasi Pembayaran',
109:                 );
110: 
111:                 $this->createRequest(
112:                     user: $user,
113:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
114:                     status: RequestStatus::PaymentInvalid,
115:                     reviewer: $admin,
116:                     rejectionReason: null,
117:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
118:                     titleSuffix: 'Pembayaran Ditolak',
119:                 );
120: 
121:                 $this->createRequest(
122:                     user: $user,
123:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
124:                     status: RequestStatus::Processing,
125:                     reviewer: $admin,
126:                     rejectionReason: null,
127:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
128:                     titleSuffix: 'Pembayaran Dikonfirmasi',
129:                 );
130: 
131:                 $this->createRequest(
132:                     user: $user,
133:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
134:                     status: RequestStatus::Expired,
135:                     reviewer: $admin,
136:                     rejectionReason: null,
137:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
138:                     titleSuffix: 'Kedaluwarsa',
139:                 );
140:             } else {
141:                 $this->createRequest(
142:                     user: $user,
143:                     purposeType: $purposeType,
144:                     status: RequestStatus::Processing,
145:                     reviewer: $admin,
146:                     rejectionReason: null,
147:                     sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
148:                     titleSuffix: 'Sedang Diproses',
149:                 );
150:             }
151: 
152:             $this->createRequest(
153:                 user: $user,
154:                 purposeType: $purposeType,
155:                 status: RequestStatus::Completed,
156:                 reviewer: $admin,
157:                 rejectionReason: null,
158:                 sourcePdfs: $sourcePdfs,
159:                 titleSuffix: 'Selesai',
160:             );
161:         }
162:     }
163: 
164:     /**
165:      * Create a single DataRequest with its associated documents.
166:      */
167:     private function createRequest(
168:         User $user,
169:         PurposeType $purposeType,
170:         RequestStatus $status,
171:         ?User $reviewer,
172:         ?string $rejectionReason,
173:         array $sourcePdfs,
174:         string $titleSuffix,
175:     ): void {
176:         $dataRequest = DataRequest::create([
177:             'request_code' => DataRequest::generateRequestCode(),
178:             'title' => "{$purposeType->label()} - {$titleSuffix}",
179:             'user_id' => $user->id,
180:             'purpose_type' => $purposeType->value,
181:             'request_category' => $purposeType->category()->value,
182:             'status' => $status,
183:             'rejection_reason' => $rejectionReason,
184:             'reviewed_by' => $reviewer?->id,
185:             'reviewed_at' => $status !== RequestStatus::Pending ? now() : null,
186:         ]);
187: 
188:         // Attach documents based on required document types
189:         $requiredDocs = $purposeType->requiredDocuments();
190: 
191:         foreach ($requiredDocs as $index => $documentType) {
192:             // Cycle through the 4 available PDFs
193:             $sourceFile = $sourcePdfs[$index % count($sourcePdfs)];
194: 
195:             // Copy the PDF into storage
196:             $storagePath = Storage::disk('local')->putFileAs(
197:                 "data-requests/{$dataRequest->id}",
198:                 new File($sourceFile),
199:                 $documentType . '.pdf'
200:             );
201: 
202:             RequestDocument::create([
203:                 'data_request_id' => $dataRequest->id,
204:                 'document_type' => $documentType,
205:                 'file_path' => $storagePath,
206:                 'original_name' => PurposeType::documentLabel($documentType) . '.pdf',
207:                 'file_size' => filesize($sourceFile),
208:             ]);
209:         }
210: 
211:         // Add dummy payment logic only for paid categories.
212:         if (
213:             $purposeType->category() === RequestCategory::Paid
214:             && in_array($status, [
215:                 RequestStatus::PaymentRequired,
216:                 RequestStatus::PaymentVerifying,
217:                 RequestStatus::PaymentInvalid,
218:                 RequestStatus::Processing,
219:                 RequestStatus::Completed,
220:             ], true)
221:         ) {
222:             Storage::disk('local')->putFileAs(
223:                 "payment_documents/invoices",
224:                 new File($sourcePdfs[0]),
225:                 "invoice_{$dataRequest->id}.pdf"
226:             );
227:             $dataRequest->update([
228:                 // 'payment_invoice_path' => $invoicePath,
229:                 // 'payment_amount' => 150000.00,
230:             ]);
231:         }
232: 
233:         // Add dummy proof if user has supposedly paid
234:         if (
235:             $purposeType->category() === RequestCategory::Paid
236:             && in_array($status, [
237:                 RequestStatus::PaymentVerifying,
238:                 RequestStatus::Processing,
239:                 RequestStatus::Completed,
240:             ], true)
241:         ) {
242:             Storage::disk('local')->putFileAs(
243:                 "payment_documents/proofs",
244:                 new File($sourcePdfs[1]),
245:                 "proof_{$dataRequest->id}.pdf"
246:             );
247:             $dataRequest->update([
248:                 // 'payment_proof_path' => $proofPath,
249:                 // 'payment_verified_at' => in_array($status, [RequestStatus::PaymentVerified, RequestStatus::Processing, RequestStatus::Completed]) ? now() : null,
250:             ]);
251:         }
252:     }
253: }

================
File: docs/activity_log_guidelines.md
================
 1: # Panduan Pencatatan Aktivitas (Audit Trail & Logging Policy)
 2: 
 3: Dokumen ini berisi aturan standar untuk semua _Software Engineer_ yang bekerja di project Web BMKG Data Request terkait bagaimana kita mencatat riwayat aktivitas (log) di dalam aplikasi.
 4: 
 5: ## Filosofi Dasar: "Jangan Log Semuanya ke Database"
 6: 
 7: Mencatat setiap aksi (seperti user login, user melihat halaman, atau setiap query `SELECT`) ke dalam database adalah bentuk **Anti-Pattern**. Hal ini akan menyebabkan:
 8: 1. Pembengkakan ukuran database secara tidak terkontrol (_Database Bloat_).
 9: 2. Penurunan performa secara drastis pada saat aplikasi sedang ramai.
10: 3. Kesulitan dalam melakukan _query_ pencarian data audit saking banyaknya data sampah.
11: 
12: ## Pendekatan yang Digunakan
13: 
14: Kita membagi sistem logging menjadi dua tipe:
15: 
16: ### 1. Targeted Audit Trail (Database-Level)
17: Pencatatan aktivitas yang bersifat krusial, kritis, dan membutuhkan pertanggungjawaban legal atau persetujuan. Kita menggunakan package **`spatie/laravel-activitylog`**.
18: 
19: **YANG WAJIB DICATAT (DI-LOG) KE DATABASE:**
20: - Perubahan status "Permintaan Data" (Draft -> Pending -> Approved/Rejected).
21: - Perubahan Hak Akses / Role (_Assignment_ atau penghapusan hak Admin/Superadmin).
22: - Tindakan penghapusan data penting (_Soft Delete_ atau _Force Delete_ pada tiket/dokumen).
23: - Perubahan data kontak atau institusi pemohon data.
24: 
25: **YANG TIDAK BOLEH DICATAT KE DATABASE:**
26: - Isi _password_ (bahkan password lama), token autentikasi, dan PIN.
27: - Aksi user membuka halaman (Page Views).
28: - Pencarian (Search queries) di halaman dashboard.
29: - Aktivitas login dan logout biasa (gunakan Application Logs untuk ini).
30: 
31: ### 2. Application Logs (File-Level)
32: Aktivitas sistem tingkat rendah, status koneksi, atau error teknis. Sistem ini menggunakan bawaan Laravel _Log driver_ (biasanya tersimpan di `storage/logs/laravel.log`).
33: 
34: **YANG WAJIB DICATAT KE FILE LOG:**
35: - Exception atau Error dari aplikasi (`try-catch` blocks).
36: - Kegagalan pengiriman Email atau notifikasi pihak ketiga.
37: - Gagal login berulang kali (indikasi _Brute Force_).
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## Cara Menggunakan (Developer Guide)
42: 
43: Untuk Model yang perubahannya ingin dicatat secara otomatis, tambahkan Trait `LogsActivity` dan atur properti `getActivitylogOptions()` sesuai standar:
44: 
45: ```php
46: namespace App\Models;
47: 
48: use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;
49: use Spatie\Activitylog\Traits\LogsActivity;
50: use Spatie\Activitylog\LogOptions;
51: 
52: class DataRequest extends Model
53: {
54:     use LogsActivity;
55: 
56:     protected $fillable = ['status', 'title', 'description'];
57: 
58:     public function getActivitylogOptions(): LogOptions
59:     {
60:         return LogOptions::defaults()
61:             ->logOnly(['status', 'title']) // Hanya log kolom yang penting
62:             ->logOnlyDirty() // Hanya catat jika value-nya benar-benar berubah
63:             ->dontSubmitEmptyLogs(); // Jangan buat entry jika tidak ada perubahan relevan
64:     }
65: }
66: ```
67: 
68: Untuk mencatat aktivitas custom yang tidak terikat pada "Perubahan Model" tertentu:
69: 
70: ```php
71: activity()
72:    ->causedBy($user) // Siapa pelakunya
73:    ->performedOn($dataRequest) // Objek apa yang dikenai aksi
74:    ->log('Admin menyetujui permintaan data dengan catatan tambahan.');
75: ```
76: 
77: **Ingat:** Selalu pertimbangkan kembali, _apakah log ini penting untuk diaudit 5 tahun dari sekarang?_ Jika tidak, simpan saja di Log File biasa.

================
File: docs/admin-context.md
================
  1: # Admin Context BMKG Data Request
  2: 
  3: ## 1. Tujuan Dokumen
  4: Dokumen ini merangkum konteks menyeluruh modul admin/superadmin pada aplikasi Web BMKG Data Request untuk kebutuhan:
  5: - Penyusunan proposal pengembangan
  6: - Penyusunan laporan progres/implementasi
  7: - Onboarding developer baru (terutama area admin)
  8: 
  9: Dokumen disusun berdasarkan audit codebase saat ini (backend + frontend + struktur route + status bisnis).
 10: 
 11: ## 2. Gambaran Sistem Admin
 12: ### 2.1 Role dan Akses
 13: - `admin`:
 14:   - Verifikasi pengajuan data
 15:   - Kelola lifecycle pembayaran
 16:   - Proses dan unggah hasil data
 17:   - Verifikasi bukti survey
 18:   - Kelola konten (katalog, FAQ, survey setting, contact setting)
 19:   - Export laporan pengajuan dan user
 20: - `superadmin`:
 21:   - Kelola user (CRUD + reset password)
 22:   - Tetap bisa masuk flow dashboard admin melalui route dashboard
 23: 
 24: ### 2.2 Entry Point Navigasi
 25: Sumber: `resources/js/Constants/nav.js`
 26: - Menu utama admin:
 27:   - `Pengajuan Data` → `admin.data-requests.index`
 28:   - `Laporan` → `admin.reports.data-requests.index`
 29:   - `Profil Saya` → `profile.edit`
 30: - Kelola konten:
 31:   - `Kelola Katalog` → `admin.catalogs.index`
 32:   - `Kelola FAQ` → `admin.faqs.index`
 33:   - `Pengaturan Survey` → `admin.survey-settings.index`
 34:   - `Kontak & Sosial Media` → `admin.contact-settings.index`
 35: - Superadmin:
 36:   - `Kelola User` → `users.index`
 37: 
 38: ## 3. Peta Modul Admin
 39: ### 3.1 Backend Controller Admin
 40: Lokasi: `app/Http/Controllers/Admin`
 41: - `DataRequestController.php` (modul paling kritikal, end-to-end workflow)
 42: - `DataRequestReportController.php`
 43: - `ExportDataRequestController.php`
 44: - `ExportUserController.php`
 45: - `CatalogController.php`
 46: - `FaqController.php`
 47: - `SurveySettingController.php`
 48: - `ContactSettingController.php`
 49: 
 50: ### 3.2 Frontend Halaman Admin
 51: Lokasi: `resources/js/Pages/Admin`
 52: - `DataRequests/Index.jsx` + `DataRequests/Show.jsx` + banyak partial panel/modals
 53: - `Catalogs/Index.jsx`
 54: - `Faqs/Index.jsx`, `Create.jsx`, `Edit.jsx`
 55: - `SurveySettings/Index.jsx`
 56: - `ContactSettings/Index.jsx`
 57: - `Reports/DataRequestExport.jsx`
 58: 
 59: ### 3.3 Route Group Admin
 60: Lokasi: `routes/web.php` pada group:
 61: - Middleware: `auth`, `verified`, `role:admin`
 62: - Prefix: `/admin`
 63: - Name prefix: `admin.`
 64: 
 65: Mencakup:
 66: - Listing/detail/verifikasi pengajuan
 67: - Upload & verifikasi pembayaran
 68: - Upload/replace/delete processed documents
 69: - Verifikasi survey
 70: - Download preview dokumen terkait request
 71: - Pengaturan survey dan contact
 72: - CRUD katalog dan FAQ
 73: - Export laporan
 74: 
 75: ## 4. Domain Workflow Pengajuan Data (Sudut Pandang Admin)
 76: ### 4.1 Status Dasar Request
 77: Sumber: `app/Enums/RequestStatus.php`
 78: - `pending`
 79: - `rejected`
 80: - `payment_required`
 81: - `payment_verifying`
 82: - `payment_invalid`
 83: - `payment_verified`
 84: - `expired`
 85: - `processing`
 86: - `completed`
 87: 
 88: ### 4.2 Status Turunan Khusus Admin
 89: Sumber: `app/Services/AdminDataRequestService.php`
 90: - `invoice_reissue_requested`
 91: - `data_processing`
 92: - `complaint`
 93: - `survey_waiting_input`
 94: - `survey_verification`
 95: - `survey_rejected`
 96: 
 97: Status turunan ini dipakai untuk:
 98: - Dashboard stat card admin
 99: - Filter list admin
100: - Label status yang lebih operasional
101: 
102: ### 4.3 Workflow Operasional Admin
103: 1. **Verifikasi pengajuan awal** (`pending`):
104:   - Aksi: setujui/tolak
105:   - Jika setujui komersial: invoice dapat diunggah
106: 2. **Pembayaran**:
107:   - `payment_required` → menunggu bukti
108:   - `payment_verifying` → admin verifikasi bukti (accept/reject)
109:   - `payment_invalid` → user unggah ulang bukti
110:   - `expired` → menunggu reissue invoice
111: 3. **Pemrosesan data**:
112:   - `processing` (+ turunan `data_processing` / `complaint`)
113:   - Admin unggah hasil data (bisa multi-file)
114: 4. **Pasca upload hasil**:
115:   - Request bisa masuk fase survei (berdasarkan required surveys)
116:   - Verifikasi bukti survey (`survey_verification`, `survey_rejected`)
117: 5. **Selesai**:
118:   - `completed` ketika requirement terpenuhi
119: 
120: ## 5. Komponen UI Kritis di DataRequests (Admin)
121: Lokasi: `resources/js/Pages/Admin/DataRequests/Partials`
122: - `RequestInfoCard.jsx` (status + tindak lanjut admin)
123: - `RequestTimeline.jsx` (alur tahap demi tahap)
124: - `VerificationPanel.jsx` (aksi approve/reject pengajuan)
125: - `PaymentPanel.jsx` (invoice/proof/payment verification)
126: - `ProcessedDataPanel.jsx` (unggah hasil data)
127: - `SurveyVerificationPanel.jsx` (review bukti survey)
128: - `ConfirmActionModal.jsx`, `PaymentVerificationModal.jsx`, `UploadInvoiceModal.jsx`
129: - `VerificationDecisionButtons.jsx` (komponen tombol setujui/tolak reusable)
130: 
131: Catatan UX:
132: - Sudah ada penyeragaman tombol keputusan verifikasi (`Setujui`/`Tolak`) lintas panel verifikasi.
133: 
134: ## 6. Modul Konten Admin
135: ### 6.1 Kelola Katalog
136: Backend:
137: - `CatalogController` + `CatalogService`
138: - Tabel:
139:   - `catalog_categories` (master kategori)
140:   - `catalog_items` (item landing page, FK ke kategori)
141: 
142: Frontend:
143: - `Admin/Catalogs/Index.jsx`
144: - `Admin/Catalogs/Partials/CatalogForm.jsx`
145: 
146: Catatan mutakhir:
147: - Fitur tambah kategori dari halaman katalog sudah dinonaktifkan.
148: - Form katalog sekarang memakai kategori existing dari master DB.
149: 
150: ### 6.2 Kelola FAQ
151: Backend:
152: - `FaqController`, `FaqService`, request validation khusus
153: - Mendukung CRUD + reorder
154: 
155: Frontend:
156: - `Admin/Faqs/Index|Create|Edit`
157: - `Partials/FaqForm.jsx`
158: 
159: ### 6.3 Pengaturan Survey
160: Backend:
161: - `SurveySettingController`
162: - `SurveySettingService::sync()` untuk update daftar survei aktif
163: 
164: Frontend:
165: - `Admin/SurveySettings/Index.jsx`
166: 
167: ### 6.4 Contact & Sosial Media
168: Backend:
169: - `ContactSettingController`
170: - `ContactSettingService::sync()`
171: 
172: Frontend:
173: - `Admin/ContactSettings/Index.jsx`
174: 
175: ## 7. Laporan & Export
176: ### 7.1 Preview Laporan
177: Backend:
178: - `DataRequestReportController@index`
179: - Menyediakan heading, status option, row preview (limit), metadata total
180: 
181: Frontend:
182: - `Admin/Reports/DataRequestExport.jsx`
183: 
184: ### 7.2 Export File
185: - `ExportDataRequestController` → download XLSX laporan pengajuan
186: - `ExportUserController` → download XLSX laporan user
187: - Dependensi: `Maatwebsite\Excel`
188: 
189: ## 8. Model, Relasi, dan Data Penting
190: ### 8.1 `DataRequest` (inti domain)
191: Lokasi: `app/Models/DataRequest.php`
192: - Relasi penting:
193:   - `user`, `reviewer`
194:   - `documents`
195:   - `invoices`, `activeInvoice`
196:   - `surveys`
197:   - `productUploads`
198: - Cast enum:
199:   - `purpose_type`, `applicant_type`, `request_category`, `status`
200: - Appended attribute:
201:   - `effective_required_surveys`
202:   - `are_surveys_completed`
203: - Scope filter admin sangat kaya (status turunan, search, date range, purpose)
204: 
205: ### 8.2 Payment Domain
206: - `InvoiceStatus`: `issued`, `verifying`, `paid`, `invalid`, `expired`
207: - OCR status invoice dipoll via endpoint JSON:
208:   - `admin.data-requests.invoices.ocr-status`
209: 
210: ### 8.3 Survey Domain
211: - `SurveyStatus`: `pending`, `approved`, `rejected`
212: - Required survey disimpan sebagai snapshot + hybrid logic agar requirement tidak “meloncat” saat setting global berubah.
213: 
214: ## 9. Security, Policy, dan Audit Trail
215: ### 9.1 Authorization
216: - Banyak aksi di `Admin\DataRequestController` memakai policy gate:
217:   - `view`, `verify`, `verifyPayment`, `process`, `uploadInvoice`, dst.
218: - FAQ juga memakai policy (`viewAny`, `create`, `update`, `delete`).
219: 
220: ### 9.2 Middleware
221: - Route admin diproteksi `auth + verified + role:admin`.
222: - Superadmin user management diproteksi `role:superadmin`.
223: 
224: ### 9.3 Activity Log
225: - Aktivitas pembayaran dan pemrosesan banyak direkam via Spatie Activitylog
226: - Event penting:
227:   - `invoice_uploaded`
228:   - `payment_proof_verified/rejected`
229:   - `processing_started`
230:   - `processed_document_replaced/deleted`
231:   - `required_surveys_updated`
232: 
233: ## 10. Kesiapan Proposal: Scope Enhancement yang Relevan
234: Rekomendasi area proposal (berbasis kondisi codebase saat ini):
235: - Standardisasi design system admin untuk semua action buttons/modals
236: - Konsolidasi status dictionary (single source untuk backend + frontend)
237: - Observability admin (dashboard event metrics + SLA tiap status)
238: - Hardening export/report (filter preset, saved report template)
239: - QA pipeline khusus admin flow (regression test untuk status turunan)
240: 
241: ## 11. Kesiapan Laporan: Struktur Pelaporan yang Disarankan
242: Template ringkas laporan implementasi:
243: 1. Latar belakang modul admin
244: 2. Cakupan fitur terimplementasi per domain (request/payment/survey/content/report)
245: 3. Alur bisnis status + keputusan admin
246: 4. Daftar endpoint admin kritikal
247: 5. Daftar komponen frontend admin kritikal
248: 6. Temuan gap UX/teknis + rencana perbaikan
249: 7. Risiko dan mitigasi
250: 8. Lampiran: peta file backend/frontend terkait
251: 
252: ## 12. Lampiran Peta File Kunci
253: Backend:
254: - `app/Http/Controllers/Admin/DataRequestController.php`
255: - `app/Services/AdminDataRequestService.php`
256: - `app/Models/DataRequest.php`
257: - `app/Enums/RequestStatus.php`
258: - `app/Enums/InvoiceStatus.php`
259: - `app/Enums/SurveyStatus.php`
260: - `routes/web.php`
261: 
262: Frontend:
263: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/DataRequests/Index.jsx`
264: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/DataRequests/Show.jsx`
265: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/DataRequests/Partials/*`
266: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/Catalogs/*`
267: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/Faqs/*`
268: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/SurveySettings/Index.jsx`
269: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/ContactSettings/Index.jsx`
270: - `resources/js/Pages/Admin/Reports/DataRequestExport.jsx`
271: - `resources/js/Constants/nav.js`
272: 
273: ---
274: 
275: Dokumen ini bisa dijadikan baseline context. Saat ada perubahan flow/status/admin module baru, update dokumen ini agar proposal dan laporan tetap sinkron dengan implementasi aktual.

  1: # Chatbot RAG Implementation Progress
  2: 
  3: > **Last Updated**: 2026-04-30  
  4: > **Status**: Phase 1-4 Complete  
  5: > **Progress**: 100% Complete
  6: 
  7: ---
  8: 
  9: ## 📊 Phase Summary
 10: 
 11: | Phase | Component | Status | Effort |
 12: |-------|-----------|--------|--------|
 13: | **1** | Knowledge Indexing | ✅ COMPLETE | 3-4 hrs |
 14: | **2** | Retrieval & Chatbot Service | ✅ COMPLETE | 5-7 hrs |
 15: | **3** | Frontend Widget | ✅ COMPLETE | 4-6 hrs |
 16: | **4** | Test Fixes & Integration | ✅ COMPLETE | 1-2 hrs |
 17: 
 18: ---
 19: 
 20: ## ✅ Phase 2 Deliverables
 21: 
 22: ### Services (9 files)
 23: | File | Purpose |
 24: |------|---------|
 25: | `app/Services/Chatbot/ChatbotService.php` | Main orchestrator with jailbreak detection |
 26: | `app/Services/Chatbot/EmbeddingService.php` | Gemini text-embedding-004 client |
 27: | `app/Services/Chatbot/VectorStoreService.php` | MySQL vector search + keyword fallback |
 28: | `app/Services/Chatbot/RetrievalService.php` | Orchestrates embed + search + ranking |
 29: | `app/Services/Chatbot/CacheService.php` | Q&A pair caching dengan semantic similarity |
 30: | `app/Services/Chatbot/PromptBuilder.php` | System prompt + security hardening |
 31: | `app/Services/Chatbot/LLMService.php` | Gemini Flash API client with retry logic |
 32: | `app/Services/Chatbot/Contracts/LLMServiceInterface.php` | Pluggable LLM contract |
 33: | `app/Services/Chatbot/Contracts/VectorStoreInterface.php` | Pluggable vector store contract |
 34: 
 35: ### Models & Database
 36: | File | Purpose |
 37: |------|---------|
 38: | `app/Models/ChatConversation.php` | Guest sesi + user conversations (ULID) |
 39: | `app/Models/ChatMessage.php` | Individual messages immutable |
 40: | `app/Models/CachedQaPair.php` | Smart FAQ cache dengan hit tracking |
 41: | `database/migrations/2026_05_01_000001_create_chat_conversations_table.php` | Conversations table |
 42: | `database/migrations/2026_05_01_000002_create_chat_messages_table.php` | Messages table |
 43: | `database/migrations/2026_05_01_000003_create_cached_qa_pairs_table.php` | Cache table |
 44: 
 45: ### Controller & Routes
 46: | File | Purpose |
 47: |------|---------|
 48: | `app/Http/Controllers/ChatbotController.php` | 5 endpoints: sendMessage, getConversation, rateMessage, submitFeedback, suggestedQuestions, status |
 49: | `routes/api.php` | RESTful endpoints dengan rate limiting |
 50: | `config/chatbot.php` | Centralized configuration |
 51: 
 52: ### Security Features
 53: - ✅ System prompt hardening (prioritas instruksi keamanan)
 54: - ✅ Strong delimiters `[BEGIN_KONTEKS]`, `[END_KONTEKS]`, etc.
 55: - ✅ 25+ jailbreak pattern detection
 56: - ✅ Input sanitization (regex-based)
 57: - ✅ Conversation & input length limits
 58: - ✅ Rate limiting per session
 59:

 61: | File | Status | Notes |
