 1: # Dashboard Wireframes & Mockups
 2: 
 3: ## Objectives
 4: - Provide role-specific dashboards (Admin vs User) using `AdminDashboard` and `UserDashboard` components.
 5: - Implement a unified layout header merging the sidebar toggle and page title.
 6: - Maintain a clean, responsive layout with a subtle topography background.
 7: 
 8: ## Layout sketch
 9: 
10: ```mermaid
11: flowchart TB
12:   subgraph APP
13:     direction TB
14:     HEADER[Unified Header (Toggle + Title)]
15:     subgraph BODY[Main Content Area]
16:       SB[Sidebar (Collapsible/Drawer)]
17:       subgraph CONTENT
18:         BG[Topography Background]
19:         ST[Stats/Trend Chart]
20:         RE[Data Tables]
21:       end
22:     end
23:   end
24:   HEADER --- BODY
25:   SB --- CONTENT
26: ```
27: 
28: ## Core components
29: - `AuthenticatedLayout`: Unified shell with merged header/sidebar logic and background.
30: - `AdminDashboard`: The main view for admins, incorporating stats and management tools.
31: - `UserDashboard`: Simplified view for standard users to track their requests.
32: - `TrendChart`: Visualizes request volume over time.
33: - `DashboardStatsCard`: Metrics display with delta indicators.
34: - `DashboardTable`: Request management list.
35: 
36: ## Behavior notes
37: - Export flows surface an alert/placeholder until the backend endpoint is wired; once ready the button can trigger `router.get(route('exports.requests'))`.
38: - User Management is tucked into the sidebar for superadmins and linked in the hero banner.
39: - Stats should update via Inertia props (`dashboard.stats`) with daily cache invalidation.
40: - Activity feed accepts 3–5 log entries; fallback text informs the admin if nothing is recorded yet.
41: 
42: ## Next steps for UI work
43: 1. Wire up the Inertia controller so `dashboard` props include `stats`, `recentRequests`, and `activity`.
44: 2. Add storybook/visual tests for the new components (optional) and sync Tailwind theme tokens.
45: 3. Apply the same shell to other admin pages (User Management, Settings) so the layout feels cohesive.
46: ```

================
File: docs/explain.md
================
  1: # Keamanan & Infrastruktur Email — BMKG Data Request
  2: 
  3: > Dokumentasi teknis tentang mekanisme keamanan yang diterapkan di proyek ini
  4: > dan cara kerja pengiriman email (SMTP Gmail vs Resend).
  5: 
  6: ---
  7: 
  8: ## Daftar Isi
  9: 
 10: 1. [Security: Lapisan-lapisan Keamanan](#1-lapisan-lapisan-keamanan)
 11: 2. [Pengiriman Email (SMTP & Resend)](#2-pengiriman-email)
 12: 
 13: ---
 14: 
 15: ## 1. Lapisan-lapisan Keamanan
 16: 
 17: Keamanan di proyek ini bukan satu fitur tunggal — dia tersebar di banyak layer.
 18: Ini breakdown-nya dari luar (HTTP) ke dalam (database).
 19: 
 20: ### 1.1 HTTP Security Headers
 21: 
 22: **File**: `app/Http/Middleware/SecurityHeaders.php`
 23: **Registrasi**: `bootstrap/app.php` → middleware web stack
 24: 
 25: Middleware ini **otomatis dipasang di semua response HTTP**. Setiap halaman yang diakses user akan dikasih header tambahan:
 26: 
 27: | Header | Nilai | Fungsi |
 28: |--------|-------|--------|
 29: | `X-Content-Type-Options` | `nosniff` | Browser gak boleh "menebak" tipe file. Kalau server bilang ini PDF, ya PDF — gak bisa di-sniff jadi HTML/JS. Mencegah serangan MIME-type confusion. |
 30: | `X-Frame-Options` | `SAMEORIGIN` | Halaman ini cuma boleh di-embed (iframe) dari domain sendiri. Mencegah **clickjacking** — dimana attacker embed halaman kita di dalam iframe-nya supaya user klik sesuatu tanpa sadar. |
 31: | `X-XSS-Protection` | `1; mode=block` | Minta browser aktifkan built-in XSS filter. Kalau terdeteksi ada reflected XSS, browser block halaman daripada render. |
 32: | `Referrer-Policy` | `strict-origin-when-cross-origin` | Kontrol data apa yang dikirim di header `Referer`. Kalau navigasi ke domain lain, cuma kirim origin (e.g. `https://bmkg.go.id`), bukan full URL. Jaga privasi path/query. |
 33: | `Permissions-Policy` | `geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()` | Matikan akses ke sensor device. Website ini gak butuh lokasi/mic/kamera, jadi di-deny semua. Mencegah malicious script exploit fitur device. |
 34: 
 35: ### 1.2 CSRF Protection
 36: 
 37: **File**: `resources/js/bootstrap.js` (frontend) + Laravel built-in (backend)
 38: 
 39: CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery) = serangan dimana website lain bikin request ke server kita atas nama user yang sedang login.
 40: 
 41: **Cara kerjanya:**
 42: 1. Laravel generate token unik per session
 43: 2. Axios (HTTP client di frontend) otomatis kirim token via cookie `XSRF-TOKEN` di setiap request POST/PUT/DELETE
 44: 3. Laravel cocokkan token — kalau gak match, return **419 (Session Expired)**
 45: 
 46: Semua form submission yang pakai Inertia `useForm()` → `post()` / `put()` / `delete()` sudah otomatis include CSRF token. Kamu gak perlu handle manual.
 47: 
 48: ### 1.3 Authentication (Siapa Kamu?)
 49: 
 50: **Stack**: Laravel Breeze (session-based) + Google OAuth
 51: 
 52: | Mekanisme | File | Penjelasan |
 53: |-----------|------|------------|
 54: | **Session Auth** | `config/session.php` | Session disimpan di database (tabel `sessions`), bukan file. Lebih aman karena server-side. Lifetime default: 120 menit. |
 55: | **Email Verification** | `User` model implements `MustVerifyEmail` | User harus verifikasi email sebelum bisa akses fitur. Route dilindungi middleware `verified`. |
 56: | **Google OAuth** | `Auth/GoogleController.php` + `HandleSocialLogin` action | Login via Google, tapi tetap harus complete profile (isi phone, dll) sebelum bisa pakai sistem. |
 57: | **Password Hashing** | `User` model: `'password' => 'hashed'` cast | Password otomatis di-hash pakai bcrypt. Gak pernah disimpan plain text. Bahkan admin gak bisa lihat password asli user. |
 58: | **Profile Completion** | `EnsureProfileIsComplete` middleware | User OAuth yang belum isi profil langsung di-redirect ke halaman complete profile. Gak bisa akses halaman lain. |
 59: 
 60: ### 1.4 Authorization (Boleh Gak?)
 61: 
 62: Setelah tau **siapa** user-nya, selanjutnya cek **boleh gak** dia akses resource tertentu.
 63: 
 64: #### Middleware Role-Based (`EnsureUserHasRole`)
 65: ```
 66: Route::middleware('role:admin')       → admin + superadmin boleh
 67: Route::middleware('role:superadmin')  → superadmin doang
 68: ```
 69: Pakai **hierarchy system**: superadmin (level 2) otomatis punya akses admin (level 1).
 70: 
 71: #### Policy (`DataRequestPolicy`, `FaqPolicy`)
 72: Aturan granular per-resource. Contoh aturan di `DataRequestPolicy`:
 73: 
 74: | Method | Siapa Boleh | Kondisi |
 75: |--------|-------------|---------|
 76: | `view` | Owner atau Admin | — |
 77: | `verify` | Admin | Request belum `completed` |
 78: | `uploadInvoice` | Admin | Status `pending` atau `payment_required` |
 79: | `uploadPaymentProof` | Owner | Status `payment_required`, `payment_invalid`, atau `expired` |
 80: | `verifyPayment` | Admin | Status `payment_verifying` |
 81: | `process` | Admin | Status `payment_verified` atau `processing` |
 82: | `requestNewInvoice` | Owner | Status `expired` |
 83: 
 84: Setiap controller method **harus** panggil `$this->authorize('method', $model)` sebelum eksekusi logic.
 85: 
 86: ### 1.5 Input Validation (FormRequest)
 87: 
 88: Semua input user **harus** divalidasi sebelum diproses. Proyek ini pakai ~17 FormRequest class.
 89: 
 90: Contoh validasi yang diterapkan:
 91: 
 92: | FormRequest | Contoh Rules |
 93: |------------|--------------|
 94: | `StoreDataRequestRequest` | `title`: required, max 255. `purpose_type`: harus enum yang valid. File: PDF only, max 5MB |
 95: | `StorePaymentProofRequest` | File: required, mimes `pdf,jpg,jpeg,png`, max 5MB |
 96: | `LoginRequest` | Email: required, valid format. Password: required. + rate limiting (5 percobaan) |
 97: | `StoreAdminRequest` | Email: unique, role: harus enum valid |
 98: | `ReviewDataRequestRequest` | Action: harus `approve`/`reject`, rejection_reason: wajib kalau reject |
 99: 
100: **Kenapa pakai FormRequest bukan validasi di controller?**
101: - Separation of concerns — validasi terpisah dari logic
102: - Reusable — bisa dipakai di banyak controller
103: - Testable — bisa di-test sendiri
104: - Authorization juga bisa di-handle di FormRequest (`authorize()` method)
105: 
106: ### 1.6 Rate Limiting
107: 
108: Mencegah abuse/brute force dengan membatasi jumlah request per waktu.
109: 
110: | Route | Limit | Kenapa |
111: |-------|-------|--------|
112: | Login | 5 percobaan, lalu lockout | Cegah brute force password |
113: | Submit Data Request | 5 req/menit | Cegah spam submission |
114: | Upload Bukti Bayar | 10 req/menit | Cegah flooding upload |
115: | Upload Invoice (admin) | 10 req/menit | Safety net |
116: | Verifikasi Payment (admin) | 10 req/menit | Safety net |
117: | Track Request (publik) | 5 req/menit | Cegah scraping |
118: | Email Verification Resend | 3 req/menit | Cegah email bombing |
119: | Password Reset | 6 req/menit | Cegah email bombing |
120: 
121: Login punya tambahan: **throttle key** pakai kombinasi `email|IP`, jadi brute force dari IP yang sama ke email yang sama bakal ke-block.
122: 
123: ### 1.7 Mass Assignment Protection
124: 
125: **File**: Semua model (`$fillable` property)
126: 
127: Laravel punya fitur mass assignment — bikin model dari array: `User::create($data)`.
128: Tanpa `$fillable`, attacker bisa inject field tambahan (misal `role` → jadi admin).
129: 
130: Setiap model **cuma allow field yang ada di `$fillable`**. Field sensitif disembunyikan:
131: ```php
132: // User model
133: protected $hidden = ['password', 'remember_token'];  // Gak pernah dikirim ke frontend
134: ```
135: 
136: ### 1.8 SQL Injection Prevention
137: 
138: Semua query pakai **Eloquent ORM** atau parameter binding. Gak ada raw SQL dengan user input langsung.
139: 
140: ```php
141: // ✅ Aman — Eloquent auto-bind parameter
142: DataRequest::where('request_code', $code)->first();
143: 
144: // ❌ BAHAYA — gak pernah dilakukan di proyek ini
145: DB::select("SELECT * FROM data_requests WHERE code = '$code'");
146: ```
147: 
148: ### 1.9 File Upload Security
149: 
150: | Aspek | Implementasi |
151: |-------|-------------|
152: | **Tipe file dibatasi** | `mimes:pdf,jpg,jpeg,png` — hanya format yang diizinkan |
153: | **Ukuran dibatasi** | Max 5MB per file |
154: | **Storage terpisah** | File disimpan di `storage/app/` (private), bukan `public/` |
155: | **Download via controller** | User harus melalui authorization check sebelum bisa download |
156: | **Cleanup on failure** | Kalau transaksi DB gagal, file yang sudah di-upload dihapus |
157: 
158: ### 1.10 Error Handling
159: 
160: **File**: `bootstrap/app.php` → `withExceptions()`
161: 
162: Di production, error message **tidak pernah expose detail internal** ke user:
163: 
164: ```php
165: // Debug mode OFF → user lihat pesan generik
166: 403 → 'Forbidden.'
167: 404 → 'Page not found.'
168: 500 → 'Server error.'
169: 
170: // Debug mode ON (development only) → tampilkan pesan exception asli
171: ```
172: 
173: Semua error di-render sebagai halaman Inertia (`Error.jsx`), bukan halaman Laravel default.
174: 
175: ### 1.11 Soft Deletes & Audit Trail
176: 
177: | Fitur | File | Penjelasan |
178: |-------|------|------------|
179: | **Soft Deletes** | `User`, `DataRequest` models | Record yang "dihapus" gak beneran hilang dari DB — cuma di-flag `deleted_at`. Bisa di-restore kalau perlu. |
180: | **Activity Log** | Spatie Activity Log (migration `create_activity_log_table`) | Setiap perubahan status request, perubahan data user, login/logout di-log. Berguna untuk audit trail. |
181: 
182: ### 1.12 Environment Security
183: 
184: - `.env` ada di `.gitignore` — **gak pernah masuk Git**
185: - Semua kredensial (DB password, API key, SMTP password) disimpan di `.env`
186: - `.env.example` cuma berisi template tanpa value sensitif
187: 
188: ---
189: 
190: ## 2. Pengiriman Email
191: 
192: Proyek ini kirim email untuk: verifikasi email, notifikasi status request (approve/reject/bayar/dll), dan password reset. Semua notifikasi **dikirim via queue** (background job, bukan blocking).
193: 
194: ### 2.1 Tiga Opsi Mail Driver
195: 
196: Di `.env.example` sudah disiapkan 3 opsi:
197: 
198: ### Opsi 1: Gmail SMTP (Aktif dipakai saat development)
199: 
200: ```env
201: MAIL_MAILER=smtp
202: MAIL_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
203: MAIL_PORT=587
204: MAIL_USERNAME=emailkamu@gmail.com
205: MAIL_PASSWORD=xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
206: MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls
207: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS="emailkamu@gmail.com"
208: MAIL_FROM_NAME="BMKG Data Request"
209: ```
210: 
211: **Cara kerjanya:**
212: 1. Aplikasi Laravel connect ke server SMTP Gmail (`smtp.gmail.com:587`)
213: 2. Autentikasi pakai email + App Password Google kamu
214: 3. Gmail relay email ke recipient
215: 4. Enkripsi pakai TLS (Transport Layer Security)
216: 
217: **Setup App Password Google:**
218: 1. Buka [Google Account > Security](https://myaccount.google.com/security)
219: 2. Aktifkan **2-Step Verification** (wajib)
220: 3. Cari menu **App Passwords** (bisa search "app password" di page settings)
221: 4. Pilih app "Mail" → generate → dapat 16-char password
222: 5. Paste di `MAIL_PASSWORD` (format: `xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx`, tanpa spasi)
223: 
224: **Kelebihan:**
225: - Gratis
226: - Setup cepat, cocok development
227: - Bisa kirim ke email siapa aja (Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook, dll)
228: - Reliable — infrastruktur Google
229: 
230: **Kekurangan:**
231: - Limit 500 email/hari (akun personal) atau 2.000/hari (Google Workspace)
232: - `From:` address harus email Gmail yang dipakai auth — gak bisa custom domain
233: - Google bisa blokir kalau terdeteksi "less secure" atau spam behavior
234: - Gak scalable untuk production dengan traffic tinggi
235: 
236: ### Opsi 2: Resend API (Untuk production)
237: 
238: ```env
239: MAIL_MAILER=resend
240: RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxxxxxxxx
241: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS="data@bmkg.go.id"
242: MAIL_FROM_NAME="BMKG Data Request"
243: ```
244: 
245: **Cara kerjanya:**
246: 1. Aplikasi kirim email via HTTP API ke Resend (bukan SMTP connection)
247: 2. Resend yang handle pengiriman actual email
248: 3. Lebih cepat karena API call biasanya lebih ringan dari SMTP handshake
249: 
250: **Setup:**
251: 1. Daftar di [resend.com](https://resend.com)
252: 2. Verify domain (e.g. `bmkg.go.id`) — butuh tambah DNS record (SPF, DKIM)
253: 3. Generate API key
254: 4. Install package: `composer require resend/resend-laravel`
255: 5. Paste API key di `RESEND_API_KEY`
256: 
257: **Kelebihan:**
258: - Bisa pakai custom domain (`data@bmkg.go.id`)
259: - Rate limit jauh lebih tinggi (100 email/detik di plan berbayar)
260: - Dashboard analytics (open rate, bounce, dll)
261: - Better deliverability — domain ter-verify punya reputasi lebih baik
262: - API-based = lebih cepat dan reliable dari SMTP
263: 
264: **Kekurangan:**
265: - Butuh **domain verification** (akses DNS setting domain)
266: - Free tier: 3.000 email/bulan (100/hari) — lebih ketat dari Gmail untuk volume kecil
267: - Dependency ke third-party service
268: - Butuh internet (untuk API call ke Resend server)
269: 
270: ### Opsi 3: Log Driver (Testing/Development tanpa email)
271: 
272: ```env
273: MAIL_MAILER=log
274: ```
275: 
276: Email gak dikirim beneran — cuma ditulis ke `storage/logs/laravel.log`. Berguna kalau cuma mau test logic tanpa harus setup SMTP.
277: 
278: ### 2.2 Perbandingan Gmail SMTP vs Resend
279: 
280: | Aspek | Gmail SMTP | Resend API |
281: |-------|-----------|------------|
282: | **Protokol** | SMTP (TCP connection, handshake) | HTTP API (REST call) |
283: | **Kecepatan** | Lebih lambat (SMTP handshake) | Lebih cepat (single HTTP request) |
284: | **From Address** | Harus Gmail yang dipakai auth | Custom domain (butuh DNS verify) |
285: | **Limit (free)** | 500/hari | 100/hari (3.000/bulan) |
286: | **Limit (paid)** | 2.000/hari (Workspace) | 100/detik (plan berbayar) |
287: | **Deliverability** | Bagus (Gmail trusted) tapi gak bisa custom domain | Sangat bagus (SPF+DKIM+DMARC) |
288: | **Setup** | Mudah (App Password) | Sedang (DNS records) |
289: | **Cocok untuk** | Development, testing | Production, high volume |
290: | **Biaya** | Gratis | Free tier + paid plan |
291: 
292: ### 2.3 Bagaimana Email Dikirim di Proyek Ini
293: 
294: Semua notifikasi implement `ShouldQueue` — artinya gak dikirim langsung, tapi masuk ke tabel `jobs` di database, lalu diproses oleh queue worker.
295: 
296: ```
297: User action (e.g. admin approve request)
298:     ↓
299: Controller panggil notifikasi:
300:     $dataRequest->user->notify(new DataRequestStatusNotification($dataRequest))
301:     ↓
302: Laravel masukkan ke tabel `jobs` (karena ShouldQueue)
303:     ↓
304: Queue worker (`php artisan queue:listen`) pick up job
305:     ↓
306: Kirim email via driver yang dikonfigurasi (SMTP/Resend/Log)
307: ```
308: 
309: **Email yang dikirim di proyek ini:**
310: 
311: | Notification | Trigger | Isi Email |
312: |-------------|---------|-----------|
313: | `QueuedVerifyEmail` | User register / verif ulang | Link verifikasi email |
314: | `DataRequestStatusNotification` | Setiap perubahan status | Pesan berbeda per status (approve, reject, bayar, dll) |
315: | `DataRequestApproved` | Admin approve (legacy) | Notif request disetujui |
316: | `DataRequestRejected` | Admin reject (legacy) | Notif request ditolak + alasan |
317: 
318: ### 2.4 Menjalankan Queue Worker
319: 
320: Email gak akan terkirim kalau queue worker gak jalan. Jalankan:
321: 
322: ```bash
323: php artisan queue:listen
324: ```
325: 
326: Atau kalau pakai `composer run dev`, queue worker sudah otomatis jalan bareng server.
327: 
328: ---
329: 
330: > **Note**: Dokumentasi ini dibuat berdasarkan state proyek per 2 April 2026.

================
File: docs/paymentknowledge.md
================
  1: # Payment Feature Knowledge Base
  2: 
  3: **Last Updated:** March 25, 2026
  4: **Status:** Implemented (v2 with Invoices System)
  5: 
  6: ## Overview
  7: This document outlines the workflow for the payment feature.
  8: The workflow bridges the gap between `PaymentRequired` (Admin approved request) and `Processing` (Admin starts work). It involves manual invoice upload by Admins and proof of payment upload by Users. It now uses a dedicated `invoices` table to track history, re-requests, and features automated expiration scheduling.
  9: 
 10: ## Workflow Status Transition
 11: 
 12: 1.  **Initial State**: `Pending` -> Admin Approves
 13: 2.  **Payment Required**: `PaymentRequired`
 14:     -   Admin uploads Invoice PDF/Image (Billing). This creates a new `Invoice` record.
 15:     -   Invoice gets `expires_at` set to 7 days from now (configurable).
 16:     -   System notifies User.
 17: 3.  **User Uploads Proof**:
 18:     -   User sees "Pay Now" / "Upload Proof" action.
 19:     -   User uploads proof of payment before `expires_at`.
 20:     -   **Status Change**: DataRequest becomes `PaymentVerifying`, Invoice becomes `Verifying`.
 21: 4.  **Wait Too Long (Expiration)**:
 22:     -   If the time passes `expires_at` and the status is still `Issued`.
 23:     -   Cron job (scheduler) marks Invoice and DataRequest as `Expired`.
 24:     -   User can request a new invoice, dropping the DataRequest back to `Pending` so admin approves again.
 25: 5.  **Admin Verification**:
 26:     -   Admin reviews proof.
 27:     -   **Action A (Valid)**: Admin marks as verified.
 28:         -   **Status Change**: DataRequest `Processing`, Invoice `Paid`.
 29:         -   The request moves straight into processing, so no extra "Start Processing" click is needed for new payment approvals.
 30:     -   **Action B (Invalid)**: Admin rejects payment.
 31:         -   **Status Change**: DataRequest `PaymentInvalid`, Invoice `Invalid`.
 32: 6.  **Processing**:
 33:     -   Requests that have valid payment proofs are already moved to `Processing` automatically.
 34:     -   The old `PaymentVerified` step is treated as legacy data only and is migrated to `Processing`.
 35: 
 36: ## Database Schema Architecture
 37: 
 38: ### `invoices` Table (History Tracking)
 39: - `data_request_id` -> BelongsTo `DataRequest`
 40: - `amount` -> decimal(12,2)
 41: - `invoice_path` -> string
 42: - `proof_path` -> string, nullable
 43: - `status` -> enum(`issued`, `verifying`, `paid`, `invalid`, `expired`)
 44: - `expires_at` -> timestamp
 45: - `verified_at` -> timestamp, nullable
 46: - `rejection_reason` -> text, nullable
 47: 
 48: *Note: The older inline fields on `data_requests` (`payment_amount`, `payment_invoice_path`, etc.) have been removed to normalize tracking.*
 49: 
 50: ## Logic Rules
 51: 
 52: - **Auto-Processing**: Valid payment leads directly to `Processing`; the invoice becomes `Paid`.
 53: - **Expiration Cron**: A scheduled task runs `hourly` via `routes/console.php` to find and fail old invoices.
 54: - **Accessing Invoice Data**: Call `$dataRequest->activeInvoice` to get the latest `Invoice` record (using `hasOne()->latestOfMany()`).
 55: - **Tracking History**: Keep invoice and proof records visible in admin detail so rejected and expired payments remain traceable.
 56: 
 57: ## Backend Implementation Details
 58: 
 59: ### Setup & Endpoints
 60: 
 61: **Admin:**
 62: - `POST /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-invoice` -> (Action: `UploadPaymentInvoice`)
 63: - `POST /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/verify-payment` -> (Action: `VerifyPaymentProof`)
 64: - `POST /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/process` -> (Action: `VerifyDataRequest`)
 65: - `GET /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-invoice/download`
 66: - `GET /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-proof/download`
 67: 
 68: **User:**
 69: - `POST /data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-proof` -> (Action: `SubmitPaymentProof`)
 70: - `POST /data-requests/{dataRequest}/request-new-invoice` -> (Action: `RequestNewInvoice`) *(When expired)*
 71: - `GET /data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-invoice/download`
 72: - `GET /data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-proof/download`
 73: 
 74: ---
 75: 
 76: ## Frontend Integration Guide
 77: 
 78: ### Prop Appendance
 79: When fetching a Single `DataRequest` (via `show`), the backend eager-loads `activeInvoice`. 
 80: Do not use `dataRequest.payment_amount`, instead use `dataRequest.active_invoice.amount`.
 81: 
 82: ### 1. Admin: Upload Billing/Invoice
 83: -   **Trigger**: Status is `PaymentRequired`.
 84: -   **Endpoint**: `POST /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-invoice`
 85: -   **Input**: `file` (mimes:pdf,jpg,jpeg,png, max:5MB), `amount` (numeric).
 86: 
 87: ### 2. User: Upload Proof
 88: -   **Trigger**: Status is `PaymentRequired` or `PaymentInvalid`.
 89: -   **Endpoint**: `POST /data-requests/{dataRequest}/payment-proof`
 90: -   **Input**: `file` (mimes:pdf,jpg,jpeg,png, max:5MB).
 91: 
 92: ### 3. User: Re-Request Invoice
 93: -   **Trigger**: Status is `Expired`.
 94: -   **Endpoint**: `POST /data-requests/{dataRequest}/request-new-invoice`
 95: -   **Input**: None. Resets request to `Pending`.
 96: 
 97: ### 4. Admin: Verify Payment
 98: -   **Trigger**: Status is `PaymentVerifying`.
 99: -   **Endpoint**: `POST /admin/data-requests/{dataRequest}/verify-payment`
100: -   **Inputs**: `action` (`accept` or `reject`), `rejection_reason` (if reject).

================
File: docs/PDF_INVOICE_EXTRACTION.md
================
 1: # Dokumentasi Ekstraksi Data PDF Invoice (Non-AI)
 2: 
 3: ## Konteks & Latar Belakang
 4: 
 5: Dokumen ini menjelaskan pendekatan yang digunakan pada backend proyek `web-bmkg-data-request` untuk mengautomasi pembacaan data (khususnya *Kode Billing*, *Tanggal Kadaluarsa*, dan *Total Disetor*) dari file PDF invoice yang masuk ke sistem.
 6: 
 7: Untuk mematuhi standar keamanan, kerahasiaan data instansi pemerintah (BMKG), serta menghindari dependensi biaya pada API pihak ketiga, sistem **tidak menggunakan AI (LLM / Vision API)** untuk ekstraksi ini. Sebaliknya, sistem menggunakan pendekatan ekstraksi teks langsung secara *native* pada file PDF yang di-*generate* oleh sistem (bukan PDF hasil scan/gambar).
 8: 
 9: ## Dependensi
10: 
11: Sistem menggunakan library PHP murni untuk pembacaan PDF, yaitu `smalot/pdfparser`. Library ini dipilih karena:
12: 1. Tidak membutuhkan instalasi modul server level OS (seperti `poppler-utils`).
13: 2. Sepenuhnya berjalan dengan PHP murni sehingga portabilitasnya tinggi.
14: 3. Stabil untuk PDF standar teks.
15: 
16: **Instalasi dependensi:**
17: ```bash
18: composer require smalot/pdfparser
19: ```
20: 
21: ## Arsitektur & Implementasi
22: 
23: Logika utama ekstraksi ditempatkan di sebuah *Service Class*:
24: `app/Services/InvoiceExtractionService.php`.
25: 
26: ### Cara Kerja Service:
27: 1. `Parser` membaca file PDF dan mengubah *binary layer*-nya menjadi string teks utuh (Raw Text).
28: 2. Teks mentah ini lalu diproses menggunakan **Regular Expression (Regex)** untuk mencari pola-pola kalimat yang umum dijumpai di struktur tagihan instansi.
29: 3. Service mengembalikan *array associative* (atau `null` jika proses gagal) berisi nilai yang ditemukan, misalnya:
30:    ```json
31:    {
32:        "kode_billing": "820260313377792",
33:        "tanggal_kadaluarsa": "25-11-2025",
34:        "total_disetor": 1500000,
35:        "raw_text": "..."
36:    }
37:    ```
38: 
39: ## Integrasi & Pengujian
40: 
41: Jika Anda atau rekan *Developer* / *AI Engineer* lain ingin mengubah aturan Regex karena ada perubahan format PDF invoice di masa mendatang, terdapat **Artisan Command** yang didedikasikan untuk melakukan *testing* terhadap file contoh:
42: 
43: ```bash
44: php artisan invoice:test-extract path/to/your/pdf_file.pdf
45: ```
46: 
47: **Contoh Command:**
48: ```bash
49: php artisan invoice:test-extract docs/820260313377792.pdf
50: ```
51: *Output command ini akan menampilkan tabel hasil nilai yang berhasil ditangkap (Extracted Value) serta teks mentah (RAW TEXT) yang didapatkan oleh `smalot`, sehingga memudahkan pembuatan Regex baru.*
52: 
53: ## Future Enhancement
54: Apabila kedepannya format tagihan tidak terstruktur atau sering berbentuk foto pindai (scan), arsitektur ini sudah siap untuk dimodifikasi dengan mengalihkan *engine* di dalam `InvoiceExtractionService` ke arah *Document AI API* (seperti Google Cloud Document AI, OpenAI GPT-4 Vision, atau Tesseract OCR). Namun untuk format PDF digital saat ini, Regex adalah solusi paling aman dan mutakhir (100% akurasi).

================
File: docs/php-instruction.md
================
 1: # 50 Command (Perintah) Penting Laravel & Ekosistem
 2: Daftar perintah *Command Line* (Terminal) yang wajib diingat untuk pengembangan aplikasi Web Data Request BMKG. Diurutkan berdasarkan kategori dan tingkat seberapa sering perintah tersebut digunakan.
 3: 
 4: ## 🟢 1. Server, Aplikasi & Optimasi (Sering Dipakai)
 5: Perintah untuk menjalankan server lokal, menyalakan mode *maintenance*, dan membersihkan *cache* (sangat berguna jika perubahan kode tidak muncul).
 6: 
 7: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
 8: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 9: | 1 | `php artisan serve` | Menjalankan server lokal PHP untuk *backend* di port 8000. |
10: | 2 | `npm run dev` / `composer run dev` | Menjalankan Vite server untuk me-render React, Tailwind, dan *Hot Reloading* frontend. |
11: | 3 | `php artisan optimize:clear` | **SANGAT PENTING!** Menghapus seluruh *cache* (config, route, view) sekaligus. Gunakan jika *error* aneh muncul. |
12: | 4 | `php artisan config:cache` | Menyimpan konfigurasi `.env` ke memori sesaat. Bikin web lebih cepat tapi `.env` baru tak terbaca sebelum di-*clear*. |
13: | 5 | `php artisan route:list` | Menampilkan seluruh daftar rute URL (GET/POST/dll) yang tersedia, beserta *Middleware* dan Nama rutenya. |
14: | 6 | `php artisan down` | Mematikan aplikasi (Mode Perawatan/Maintenance). Semua user melihat tulisan "Under Maintenance". |
15: | 7 | `php artisan up` | Menyalakan kembali aplikasi dari mode *maintenance*. |
16: | 8 | `php artisan key:generate` | Membuat kode kriptografi aman di `.env` (`APP_KEY`). Wajib di-*run* saat pertama kali *clone project*. |
17: | 9 | `php artisan tinker` | Masuk ke terminal bawaan (*REPL*) untuk menjalankan kode PHP/Eksperimen *database* secara langsung tanpa *Controller*. |
18: | 10 | `php artisan storage:link` | Membuat *shortcut/symlink* dari folder rahasia `storage/app/public` agar bisa diakses lewat web `public/storage`. |
19: | 11 | `php artisan info` | Melihat versi PHP, versi Laravel, dan status koneksi *database* di server secara ringkas (Laravel 11+). |
20: | 12 | `npm run build` | Melakukan *compile* UI React & Tailwind menjadi file statis super kecil untuk di-*upload* ke *Production Server*. |
21: | 13 | `composer install` | Mengunduh seluruh pustaka *Vendor* PHP sesuai berkas `composer.json` (saat baru *clone* repository). |
22: | 14 | `npm install` | Mengunduh pustaka *Node Modules* untuk keperluan *Frontend* (React/Tailwind/Vite). |
23: 
24: ## 🔵 2. Database & Migrasi (Manajemen Tabel)
25: Perintah untuk merombak struktur tabel SQL dan memasukkan data tiruan (*dumy data*).
26: 
27: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
28: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
29: | 15 | `php artisan migrate` | Menjalankan *script* migrasi untuk membuat tabel baru di *database* SQL yang belum ada (Penting dicoba tiap ada fitur). |
30: | 16 | `php artisan migrate:fresh` | **HATI-HATI!** Menghapus total seluruh isi *database* dan mengulang dari nol pembuatan tabelnya. |
31: | 17 | `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` | Menghapus keseluruhan tabel, membuatnya dari nol, dan **langsung** mengisinya dengan data tiruan (Akun Admin dsb). |
32: | 18 | `php artisan migrate:rollback` | Membatalkan langkah *migrate* terakhir (berguna jika salah ketik tipe kolom pada migrasi yang baru saja di-*run*). |
33: | 19 | `php artisan migrate:status` | Mengecek *file* migrasi mana saja yang sudah berhasil masuk ke *database* dan mana yang belum. |
34: | 20 | `php artisan db:seed` | Menjalankan kelas *DatabaseSeeder* untuk menyuntikkan data *dummy* secara manual tanpa harus menghapus tabel. |
35: | 21 | `php artisan db:seed --class=User...`| Menjalankan seeder spefisik (contoh: `UserSeeder`) jika kita hanya ingin menambah puluhan pengguna. |
36: 
37: ## 🟣 3. Make / Generator (Pembuat Otomatis File)
38: Ketimbang klik kanan tambah file, *Artisan Make* akan membuatkan *file* dengan struktur (Template) yang sudah benar dan tidak *typo*.
39: 
40: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
41: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
42: | 22 | `php artisan make:model NamaModel` | Membuat kerangka file Model Eloquent (biasanya ditaruh di `app/Models`). |
43: | 23 | `php artisan make:model Data -mrc` | **SUPER KOMBO!** Membuat Model `Data`, beserta *Migration* (-m), *Controller* (-c), dan *Resources* (-r) sekaligus. |
44: | 24 | `php artisan make:controller Nama` | Membuat *Controller* baru kosong di folder `app/Http/Controllers`. |
45: | 25 | `php artisan make:request NamaReq` | Membuat berkas Validasi (*FormRequest*) khusus, tempat aturan *password*, nama, dll dicatat (`app/Http/Requests/`). |
46: | 26 | `php artisan make:migration create_`| Membuat berkas perubahan *database* (Struktur kolom dsb). |
47: | 27 | `php artisan make:seeder NamaSeed` | Membuat berkas generator data tiruan (*Dummy Database Injector*). |
48: | 28 | `php artisan make:factory NamaFact` | Teman sejati *Seeder*, digunakan buat merancang struktur isian nama, alamat, kota fiktif (Faker). |
49: | 29 | `php artisan make:middleware NamaM` | Membuat filter penengah keamanan, misal "Cek apakah User ini PNS atau Mahasiswa". |
50: | 30 | `php artisan make:job NamaJob` | Membuat *Job* yang ringan, misal fungsi "Buat PDF Permohonan" menggunakan program latar belakang. |
51: | 31 | `php artisan make:mail KirimResi` | Membuat kerangka dasar dan *markup template* HTML otomatis pengiriman *Email* (Mailable). |
52: | 32 | `php artisan make:notification Notif`| Sama dengan Mail, tapi ini spesifik terintegrasi antrian (Queue) dan basis data notifikasi bawaan Laravel. |
53: | 33 | `php artisan make:event NamaEvent` | Membuat event (Contoh: `UserLoggedIn`, `RequestAccepted`). |
54: | 34 | `php artisan make:listener NamaList` | Menangkap dan mengeksekusi aksi nyata ketika sebuah *Event* di atas terpanggil. |
55: | 35 | `php artisan make:command NamaCmd` | Membuat perintah terminal milik kamu sendiri (Contoh `php artisan bmkg:hitung-hujan`). |
56: 
57: ## 🟠 4. Antrean Pekerjaan & Background (Queue / Scheduled)
58: Berhubungan erat dengan pengiriman email dan hapus data otomatis (Ghost Sweeper) di malam hari agar web tak *Lag*.
59: 
60: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
61: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
62: | 36 | `php artisan queue:work` | Menjalankan *worker* di *background*. PENTING untuk mengirim Email Verifikasi/Reset Password secara mulus. |
63: | 37 | `php artisan queue:restart` | Me-*restart* seluruh antrean yang sedang berjalan. Berguna jika kamu baru saja mengganti *coding* di Model/Mail. |
64: | 38 | `php artisan queue:failed` | Mengecek pekerjaan belakang layar manakah (misal kirim email) yang macet atau gagal diproses oleh server. |
65: | 39 | `php artisan queue:retry all` | Mengulang kembali paksa pekerjaan yang terlanjur tertulis di status *failed*. |
66: | 40 | `php artisan schedule:work` | (Khusus Lokal) Memaksa sistem *cronjob* berlari di terminal lokal setiap 1 menit (Misalnya mengecek sweeper akun mati).|
67: | 41 | `php artisan schedule:run` | (Khusus Production) Command yang ditaruh di dalam crontab Linux Server untuk di-*ping* OS secara otomatis tiap detik. |
68: | 42 | `php artisan schedule:list` | Mengecek dan melist seluruh jadwal pembersihan, eksekusi, dll yang saat ini tercatat di `routes/console.php`. |
69: 
70: ## 🔴 5. Keamanan & Pengujian (Security & Testing)
71: Perintah ini lazim dipakai sewaktu audit kode.
72: 
73: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
74: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
75: | 43 | `php artisan test` | Menjalankan program pengetesan *backend* (Menarik *script* buatan mesin untuk menjajal fungsi *login* dkk). |
76: | 44 | `php artisan test --parallel` | Tes di atas tapi berjalan secara *multi-threading* sehingga kelar dalam kedipan mata (Butuh RAM tinggi). |
77: | 45 | `vendor/bin/pest` | (Alternatif) Menjalankan pengetesan dengan format *framework* "Pest" yang sangat populer di ekosistem kekinian. |
78: | 46 | `php artisan sail up` | Khusus jika menggunakan lingkungan **Docker** (mesin isolasi tanpa *install* XAMPP). |
79: 
80: ## ⚪ 6. Paket Pihak Ketiga & Komponen Custom
81: 
82: | No | Perintah Command | Kegunaan / Deskripsi |
83: |----|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
84: | 47 | `php artisan lang:publish` | Mengekspor keranjang bahasa `lang/en` yang ngumpet menjadi terbuka sehingga bisa kalian ganti isinya dengan id (Indonesia). |
85: | 48 | `php artisan vendor:publish` | Perintah universal jikalau menginstal pustaka luar baru (seperti *Spatie / Activity Log*) buat menarik *script* konfigurasinya keluar. |
86: | 49 | `composer require [nama-paket]` | Menginstal alat buatan komunitas, layaknya AppStore di iOS khusus PHP. (Contoh: `composer require pestphp/pest`). |
87: | 50 | `composer update` | **HATI HATI!** Digunakan untuk mestimulasi pembaruan keseluruhan paket secara massal ke versi rilis mutakhirnya. |

================
File: docs/resend_migration.md
================
 1: # Panduan Migrasi Email: Gmail SMTP → Resend
 2: 
 3: ## Status Saat Ini: Gmail SMTP
 4: 
 5: App sekarang pakai Gmail SMTP untuk kirim email. Ini cukup untuk development & testing, tapi **tidak cocok untuk production**.
 6: 
 7: ---
 8: 
 9: ## Kapan Harus Pindah ke Resend?
10: 
11: Pindah ke Resend **sebelum deploy ke production**, karena:
12: - Gmail melarang automated email (bisa di-ban)
13: - Email dari `@gmail.com` tidak profesional untuk app pemerintah
14: - Rate limit Gmail cuma 500/hari
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## Cara Migrasi (5 menit)
19: 
20: ### Step 1: Daftar Resend
21: 1. Buka https://resend.com → Sign up
22: 2. Masuk dashboard → klik **API Keys** di sidebar
23: 3. Klik **Create API Key** → copy key-nya (format: `re_xxxxxxxxx`)
24: 
25: ### Step 2: Verify Domain (agar bisa kirim ke siapa aja)
26: 1. Di dashboard Resend → klik **Domains** di sidebar
27: 2. Klik **Add Domain** → masukkan domain (misal `bmkg.go.id`)
28: 3. Resend kasih 3 DNS records (TXT, MX, DKIM)
29: 4. Tambahkan records itu di DNS manager domain
30: 5. Tunggu verification (biasanya 5-30 menit)
31: 6. Status berubah jadi ✅ Verified
32: 
33: ### Step 3: Ganti `.env`
34: 
35: **Hapus/comment baris Gmail:**
36: ```env
37: # MAIL_MAILER=smtp
38: # MAIL_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
39: # MAIL_PORT=587
40: # MAIL_USERNAME=emailkamu@gmail.com
41: # MAIL_PASSWORD=xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
42: # MAIL_ENCRYPTION=tls
43: ```
44: 
45: **Tambah baris Resend:**
46: ```env
47: MAIL_MAILER=resend
48: RESEND_API_KEY=re_paste_key_kamu_disini
49: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS="data@bmkg.go.id"
50: MAIL_FROM_NAME="BMKG Data Request"
51: ```
52: 
53: ### Step 4: Test
54: ```bash
55: php artisan tinker
56: ```
57: ```php
58: Mail::raw('Test Resend!', fn ($m) => $m->to('email@test.com')->subject('Test'));
59: ```
60: 
61: **Selesai.** Tidak ada perubahan code. Cuma `.env`.
62: 
63: ---
64: 
65: ## Troubleshooting
66: 
67: | Error | Solusi |
68: |-------|--------|
69: | `Request to Resend API failed` | Cek RESEND_API_KEY sudah benar |
70: | `can only send to your own email` | Domain belum verified → verify di resend.com/domains |
71: | `invalid from address` | MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS harus pakai domain yang sudah verified |
72: 
73: ## Package yang Dibutuhkan
74: 
75: Sudah terinstall:
76: ```
77: composer require resend/resend-laravel
78: ```
79: Config sudah ada di `config/services.php` → `resend.key`.

================
File: docs/theme_architecture.md
================
  1: # Design System & Theme Guide
  2: 
  3: **For Frontend Developers**
  4: 
  5: This document explains how the design system is organized in this project and what goes where.
  6: 
  7: ---
  8: 
  9: ## 📁 File Structure Overview
 10: 
 11: ```
 12: resources/js/
 13: ├── Constants/              # App constants (statuses, config)
 14: │   ├── index.js           # Central export file
 15: │   ├── statuses.js        # Status enums, labels, colors
 16: │   └── config.js          # App-wide configuration
 17: ├── Utils/                  # Helper functions
 18: │   ├── formatters.js      # Date, currency, file size formatting
 19: │   ├── validators.js      # Validation functions
 20: │   └── helpers.js         # General utilities
 21: ├── Components/             # Reusable UI components
 22: │   ├── PrimaryButton.jsx
 23: │   ├── Modal.jsx
 24: │   └── ...
 25: ├── Pages/                  # Page components with Partials/
 26: └── Layouts/                # Layout wrappers
 27: 
 28: tailwind.config.js          # Tailwind theme configuration
 29: ```
 30: 
 31: ---
 32: 
 33: ## 🎨 Design System: What Goes Where?
 34: 
 35: ### **1. Colors, Typography, Spacing → `tailwind.config.js`**
 36: 
 37: **When to use:** Visual styles that need to work as Tailwind CSS classes.
 38: 
 39: ```js
 40: // tailwind.config.js
 41: export default {
 42:     theme: {
 43:         extend: {
 44:             colors: {
 45:                 // Brand colors - use as: bg-bmkg-primary, text-bmkg-secondary
 46:                 bmkg: {
 47:                     primary: '#000000',           // Hitam — warna utama brand
 48:                     secondary: '#0C2B4E',         // Navy gelap — teks/elemen sekunder
 49:                     accent: '#FFC300',            // Kuning — aksentuasi, highlight
 50:                     accent_secondary: '#FFC300',  // Alias accent (jika perlu varian)
 51:                     dark: '#0F172A',              // Slate 900 — heading, teks gelap
 52:                     light: '#F0F9FF',             // Sky 50 — background terang
 53:                 },
 54:             },
 55:             fontFamily: {
 56:                 sans: ['Inter', ...defaultTheme.fontFamily.sans],
 57:             },
 58:         },
 59:     },
 60: };
 61: ```
 62: 
 63: **Usage in components:**
 64: ```jsx
 65: <div className="bg-bmkg-primary text-white">
 66:     <h1 className="text-5xl font-bold text-bmkg-dark">BMKG Data</h1>
 67: </div>
 68: 
 69: {/* Background terang */}
 70: <section className="bg-bmkg-light">
 71:     <span className="text-bmkg-accent">Highlight</span>
 72: </section>
 73: ```
 74: 
 75: ---
 76: 
 77: ### **2. Status Enums & Labels → `Constants/statuses.js`**
 78: 
 79: **When to use:** Status values, labels, and Tailwind class combinations for JavaScript logic.
 80: 
 81: ```js
 82: // Constants/statuses.js
 83: export const REQUEST_STATUS = {
 84:     PENDING: 'pending',
 85:     APPROVED: 'approved',
 86:     REJECTED: 'rejected',
 87: };
 88: 
 89: export const REQUEST_STATUS_LABELS = {
 90:     [REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING]: 'Pending Review',
 91:     [REQUEST_STATUS.APPROVED]: 'Approved',
 92:     [REQUEST_STATUS.REJECTED]: 'Rejected',
 93: };
 94: 
 95: export const REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS = {
 96:     [REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING]: 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800 border-yellow-200',
 97:     [REQUEST_STATUS.APPROVED]: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800 border-green-200',
 98:     [REQUEST_STATUS.REJECTED]: 'bg-red-100 text-red-800 border-red-200',
 99: };
100: 
101: export const REQUEST_STATUS_ICONS = {
102:     [REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING]: '⏳',
103:     [REQUEST_STATUS.APPROVED]: '✅',
104:     [REQUEST_STATUS.REJECTED]: '❌',
105: };
106: ```
107: 
108: **Usage:**
109: ```jsx
110: import { REQUEST_STATUS, REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS, REQUEST_STATUS_ICONS } from '@/Constants';
111: 
112: function StatusBadge({ status }) {
113:     return (
114:         <span className={`px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS[status]}`}>
115:             {REQUEST_STATUS_ICONS[status]} {REQUEST_STATUS_LABELS[status]}
116:         </span>
117:     );
118: }
119: 
120: // Validation in forms
121: if (status === REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING) {
122:     // Allow editing
123: }
124: ```
125: 
126: ---
127: 
128: ### **3. App Configuration → `Constants/config.js`**
129: 
130: **When to use:** Application-wide settings, limits, options.
131: 
132: ```js
133: // Constants/config.js
134: export const FILE_UPLOAD = {
135:     MAX_SIZE: 10 * 1024 * 1024, // 10MB
136:     ALLOWED_TYPES: ['pdf', 'doc', 'docx', 'xlsx'],
137: };
138: 
139: export const PAGINATION = {
140:     DEFAULT_PER_PAGE: 15,
141:     PER_PAGE_OPTIONS: [10, 15, 25, 50],
142: };
143: 
144: export const DATA_TYPES = {
145:     METEOROLOGY: 'meteorology',
146:     GEOPHYSICS: 'geophysics',
147:     MARITIME: 'maritime',
148: };
149: ```
150: 
151: **Usage:**
152: ```jsx
153: import { FILE_UPLOAD, DATA_TYPES } from '@/Constants';
154: 
155: function FileUpload() {
156:     const handleFile = (file) => {
157:         if (file.size > FILE_UPLOAD.MAX_SIZE) {
158:             alert('File too large!');
159:         }
160:     };
161: }
162: ```
163: 
164: ---
165: 
166: ### **4. Helper Functions → `Utils/`**
167: 
168: **When to use:** Reusable formatting, validation, or utility functions.
169: 
170: #### **Formatters (`Utils/formatters.js`)**
171: ```js
172: import { formatDate, formatCurrency, formatFileSize } from '@/Utils/formatters';
173: 
174: // Usage
175: <p>{formatDate(request.created_at)}</p>
176: <p>{formatCurrency(1500000)}</p> // Output: Rp 1.500.000
177: <p>{formatFileSize(10485760)}</p> // Output: 10 MB
178: ```
179: 
180: #### **Validators (`Utils/validators.js`)**
181: ```js
182: import { validateEmail, validatePhone } from '@/Utils/validators';
183: 
184: const handleSubmit = () => {
185:     if (!validateEmail(email)) {
186:         setError('Invalid email format');
187:     }
188: };
189: ```
190: 
191: #### **Helpers (`Utils/helpers.js`)**
192: ```js
193: import { cn, truncate, chunk } from '@/Utils/helpers';
194: 
195: // Combine class names
196: <button className={cn('btn', isActive && 'active', 'text-white')}>
197: 
198: // Truncate text
199: <p>{truncate(description, 100)}</p>
200: 
201: // Split array into chunks
202: const pages = chunk(items, 10);
203: ```
204: 
205: ---
206: 
207: ## 🎯 Decision Matrix: Quick Reference
208: 
209: | What You Need | Where to Put It | Import From |
210: |---------------|-----------------|-------------|
211: | **Colors for CSS** | `tailwind.config.js` → `colors.bmkg.primary` | Use as: `bg-bmkg-primary` |
212: | **Dark text color** | `tailwind.config.js` → `colors.bmkg.dark` | Use as: `text-bmkg-dark` |
213: | **Accent highlight** | `tailwind.config.js` → `colors.bmkg.accent` | Use as: `text-bmkg-accent`, `border-bmkg-accent` |
214: | **Light background** | `tailwind.config.js` → `colors.bmkg.light` | Use as: `bg-bmkg-light` |
215: | **Typography for CSS** | `tailwind.config.js` → `fontFamily.sans` | Default Inter font |
216: | **Status enum values** | `Constants/statuses.js` → `REQUEST_STATUS` | `import { REQUEST_STATUS } from '@/Constants'` |
217: | **Status labels/colors** | `Constants/statuses.js` → `REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS` | Same import, use in `className` |
218: | **File size limits** | `Constants/config.js` → `FILE_UPLOAD.MAX_SIZE` | `import { FILE_UPLOAD } from '@/Constants'` |
219: | **Data type options** | `Constants/config.js` → `DATA_TYPES` | `import { DATA_TYPES } from '@/Constants'` |
220: | **Contact info** | `Constants/config.js` → `CONTACT` | `import { CONTACT } from '@/Constants'` |
221: | **Date formatting** | `Utils/formatters.js` → `formatDate()` | `import { formatDate } from '@/Utils/formatters'` |
222: | **Email validation** | `Utils/validators.js` → `validateEmail()` | `import { validateEmail } from '@/Utils/validators'` |
223: | **Text truncation** | `Utils/helpers.js` → `truncate()` | `import { truncate } from '@/Utils/helpers'` |
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: ## 📝 Common Patterns
228: 
229: ### **Pattern 1: Status Badge Component**
230: ```jsx
231: import { REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS, REQUEST_STATUS_LABELS, REQUEST_STATUS_ICONS } from '@/Constants';
232: 
233: export default function StatusBadge({ status }) {
234:     return (
235:         <span className={`inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS[status]}`}>
236:             <span>{REQUEST_STATUS_ICONS[status]}</span>
237:             <span>{REQUEST_STATUS_LABELS[status]}</span>
238:         </span>
239:     );
240: }
241: ```
242: 
243: ### **Pattern 2: Date Display**
244: ```jsx
245: import { formatDate, formatDateTime } from '@/Utils/formatters';
246: 
247: export default function RequestCard({ request }) {
248:     return (
249:         <div>
250:             <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">
251:                 Created: {formatDate(request.created_at, 'long')}
252:             </p>
253:             <p className="text-xs text-gray-400">
254:                 {formatDateTime(request.created_at)}
255:             </p>
256:         </div>
257:     );
258: }
259: ```
260: 
261: ### **Pattern 3: File Upload Validation**
262: ```jsx
263: import { FILE_UPLOAD } from '@/Constants';
264: import { validateFileType, validateFileSize, formatFileSize } from '@/Utils';
265: 
266: export default function FileUpload() {
267:     const handleFile = (file) => {
268:         if (!validateFileType(file, FILE_UPLOAD.ALLOWED_TYPES)) {
269:             alert(`Only ${FILE_UPLOAD.ALLOWED_TYPES.join(', ')} files allowed`);
270:             return;
271:         }
272:         
273:         if (!validateFileSize(file, FILE_UPLOAD.MAX_SIZE)) {
274:             alert(`File must be smaller than ${formatFileSize(FILE_UPLOAD.MAX_SIZE)}`);
275:             return;
276:         }
277:         
278:         // Upload file
279:     };
280: }
281: ```
282: 
283: ### **Pattern 4: Dynamic Styling**
284: ```jsx
285: import { cn } from '@/Utils/helpers';
286: import { REQUEST_STATUS } from '@/Constants';
287: 
288: export default function RequestRow({ request, isSelected }) {
289:     return (
290:         <tr className={cn(
291:             'hover:bg-gray-50',
292:             isSelected && 'bg-blue-50',
293:             request.status === REQUEST_STATUS.URGENT && 'border-l-4 border-red-500'
294:         )}>
295:             {/* ... */}
296:         </tr>
297:     );
298: }
299: ```
300: 
301: ---
302: 
303: ## 🚀 Getting Started
304: 
305: ### **Step 1: Import What You Need**
306: ```jsx
307: // Import constants
308: import { REQUEST_STATUS, DATA_TYPES } from '@/Constants';
309: 
310: // Import utilities
311: import { formatDate, formatCurrency } from '@/Utils/formatters';
312: import { validateEmail } from '@/Utils/validators';
313: import { cn, truncate } from '@/Utils/helpers';
314: ```
315: 
316: ### **Step 2: Use Tailwind Config**
317: ```jsx
318: // Use custom colors from tailwind.config.js
319: <div className="bg-bmkg-primary text-white">
320:     <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold">BMKG</h1>
321: </div>
322: ```
323: 
324: ### **Step 3: Avoid Magic Strings**
325: ```jsx
326: // ✗ BAD: Magic strings
327: if (status === 'pending') { }
328: 
329: // ✓ GOOD: Use constants
330: import { REQUEST_STATUS } from '@/Constants';
331: if (status === REQUEST_STATUS.PENDING) { }
332: ```
333: 
334: ---
335: 
336: ## 🎨 Adding New Design Tokens
337: 
338: ### **Adding New Color**
339: 
340: 1. **Add to Tailwind config:**
341: ```js
342: // tailwind.config.js → theme.extend.colors.bmkg
343: bmkg: {
344:     // ...existing colors
345:     danger: '#DC2626', // New color
346: }
347: ```
348: 
349: 2. **Use in components:**
350: ```jsx
351: <button className="bg-bmkg-danger text-white">Delete</button>
352: ```
353: 
354: > **Note:** Selalu gunakan token `bmkg.*` dari Tailwind config, hindari hardcode warna seperti `text-blue-500` atau `bg-slate-900` untuk elemen brand.
355: 
356: ### **Adding New Status**
357: 
358: 1. **Add to Constants:**
359: ```js
360: // Constants/statuses.js
361: export const REQUEST_STATUS = {
362:     // ... existing
363:     ARCHIVED: 'archived', // New status
364: };
365: 
366: export const REQUEST_STATUS_COLORS = {
367:     // ... existing
368:     [REQUEST_STATUS.ARCHIVED]: 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-600',
369: };
370: ```
371: 
372: 2. **TypeScript support (future):**
373: ```ts
374: // Will auto-complete REQUEST_STATUS.ARCHIVED
375: ```
376: 
377: ---
378: 
379: ## 📚 Additional Resources
380: 
381: - **Tailwind CSS Docs**: https://tailwindcss.com/docs
382: - **Customizing Tailwind**: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme
383: - **Project Guidelines**: See `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
384: 
385: ---
386: 
387: ## ❓ FAQ
388: 
389: **Q: Should I put colors in Constants or Tailwind config?**  
390: A: **Tailwind config** for CSS classes. **Constants** only if you need the value in JavaScript logic (rare).
391: 
392: **Q: Where do I put data type options for a select dropdown?**  
393: A: **Constants/config.js** → `DATA_TYPES`, `DATA_TYPE_LABELS`
394: 
395: **Q: How do I format a date consistently?**  
396: A: **Utils/formatters.js** → `formatDate(date, 'short')` or `'long'`
397: 
398: **Q: Can I add custom utility functions?**  
399: A: **Yes!** Add to `Utils/helpers.js` or create a new file in `Utils/` folder.
400: 
401: **Q: What if I need theme switching (light/dark mode)?**  
402: A: Use Tailwind's `dark:` variant. Example: `bg-white dark:bg-gray-900`
403: 
404: ---
405: 
406: **Happy coding! 🚀**

================
File: fix_faq.php
================
 1: <?php
 2: require "vendor/autoload.php";
 3: $app = require_once "bootstrap/app.php";
 4: $kernel = $app->make(Illuminate\Contracts\Console\Kernel::class);
 5: $kernel->bootstrap();
 6: 
 7: use App\Models\Faq;
 8: 
 9: $faqs = Faq::all();
10: $count = 0;
11: 
12: foreach ($faqs as $faq) {
13:     $originalQ = $faq->question;
14:     $originalA = $faq->answer;
15:     
16:     $newQ = str_ireplace(["permohonan", "pemohonan"], "pengajuan", $originalQ);
17:     $newA = str_ireplace(["permohonan", "pemohonan"], "pengajuan", $originalA);
18:     
19:     $newQ = str_ireplace("pemohon", "pengaju", $newQ);
20:     $newA = str_ireplace("pemohon", "pengaju", $newA);
21: 
22:     if ($originalQ !== $newQ || $originalA !== $newA) {
23:         $faq->question = $newQ;
24:         $faq->answer = $newA;
25:         $faq->save();
26:         
27:         \App\Jobs\SyncFaqKnowledge::dispatch($faq->getKey());
28:         $count++;
29:     }
30: }
31: 
32: echo "Berhasil memperbarui dan me-reindex $count FAQ!\n";

  4:     'accepted' => ':attribute harus disetujui.',

 10:     'alpha_num' => ':attribute hanya boleh berisi huruf dan angka.',
 11:     'array' => ':attribute harus berupa array.',
 12:     'before' => ':attribute harus berupa tanggal sebelum :date.',
 13:     'before_or_equal' => ':attribute harus berupa tanggal sebelum atau sama dengan :date.',
 14:     'between' => [
 15:         'numeric' => ':attribute harus antara :min dan :max.',
 16:         'file' => ':attribute harus antara :min dan :max kilobyte.',
 17:         'string' => ':attribute harus antara :min dan :max karakter.',
 18:         'array' => ':attribute harus memiliki antara :min dan :max item.',
 19:     ],
 20:     'boolean' => 'Kolom :attribute harus berupa true atau false.',

 22:     'current_password' => 'Kata sandi salah.',
 23:     'date' => ':attribute bukan tanggal yang valid.',
 24:     'date_equals' => ':attribute harus berupa tanggal yang sama dengan :date.',
 25:     'date_format' => ':attribute tidak cocok dengan format :format.',
 26:     'different' => ':attribute dan :other harus berbeda.',
 27:     'digits' => ':attribute harus berupa :digits digit.',
 28:     'digits_between' => ':attribute harus antara :min dan :max digit.',
 29:     'dimensions' => ':attribute memiliki dimensi gambar yang tidak valid.',
 30:     'distinct' => 'Kolom :attribute memiliki nilai duplikat.',
 31:     'email' => ':attribute harus berupa alamat email yang valid.',
 32:     'ends_with' => ':attribute harus diakhiri dengan salah satu dari berikut ini: :values.',
 33:     'exists' => ':attribute yang dipilih tidak valid.',

File: phpunit.xml

 1: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
 2: <phpunit xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
 3:          xsi:noNamespaceSchemaLocation="vendor/phpunit/phpunit/phpunit.xsd"
 4:          bootstrap="vendor/autoload.php"
 5:          colors="true"
 6: >
 7:     <testsuites>
 8:         <testsuite name="Unit">
 9:             <directory>tests/Unit</directory>
10:         </testsuite>
11:         <testsuite name="Feature">
12:             <directory>tests/Feature</directory>
13:         </testsuite>
14:     </testsuites>
15:     <source>
16:         <include>
17:             <directory>app</directory>
18:         </include>
19:     </source>
20:     <php>
21:         <env name="APP_ENV" value="testing"/>
22:         <env name="APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER" value="file"/>
23:         <env name="BCRYPT_ROUNDS" value="4"/>
24:         <env name="BROADCAST_CONNECTION" value="null"/>
25:         <env name="CACHE_STORE" value="array"/>
26:         <env name="DB_CONNECTION" value="sqlite"/>
27:         <env name="DB_DATABASE" value=":memory:"/>
28:         <env name="MAIL_MAILER" value="array"/>
29:         <env name="QUEUE_CONNECTION" value="sync"/>
30:         <env name="SESSION_DRIVER" value="array"/>
31:         <env name="PULSE_ENABLED" value="false"/>
32:         <env name="TELESCOPE_ENABLED" value="false"/>
33:         <env name="NIGHTWATCH_ENABLED" value="false"/>
34:     </php>
35: </phpunit>

================
File: README.md
================
  1: # BMKG Data Request System
  2: 
  3: Web application for requesting meteorology, geophysics, and maritime data from BMKG (Badan Meteorologi, Klimatologi, dan Geofisika).
  4: 
  5: ## Tech Stack
  6: 
  7: - **Backend**: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js
  8: - **Frontend**: React 18 + Tailwind CSS 4
  9: - **Auth**: Laravel Breeze (session-based)
 10: - **Database**: MySQL
 11: 
 12: ## Quick Setup
 13: 
 14: **Option 1: Automated (One Command)**
 15: ```bash
 16: composer run setup
 17: ```
 18: Automatically installs dependencies, copies `.env`, generates key, and runs migrations.
 19: 
 20: **Option 2: Manual (Step by Step)**
 21: ```bash
 22: # Install PHP dependencies
 23: composer install
 24: 
 25: # Install Node dependencies
 26: npm install
 27: 
 28: # Create environment file
 29: cp .env.example .env  # Mac/Linux
 30: copy .env.example .env  # Windows
 31: 
 32: # Generate application key
 33: php artisan key:generate
 34: 
 35: # Configure database in .env (see Environment Setup below)
 36: 
 37: # Run migrations
 38: php artisan migrate
 39: ```
 40: 
 41: ## Start Development
 42: 
 43: **Option 1: Multiple Terminals (Windows/Recommended)**
 44: ```bash
 45: # Terminal 1
 46: php artisan serve
 47: 
 48: # Terminal 2
 49: npm run dev
 50: ```
 51: 
 52: **Option 2: Single Terminal (Linux/Mac Only)**
 53: ```bash
 54: composer run dev
 55: ```
 56: ⚠️ **Note**: `composer run dev` requires `pcntl` extension (not available on Windows). Use Option 1 on Windows.
 57: 
 58: Visit: `http://localhost:8000`
 59: 
 60: ## Development Commands
 61: 
 62: ### Testing
 63: 
 64: **Option 1: Laravel Test Runner (Recommended)**
 65: ```bash
 66: php artisan test
 67: ```
 68: ✅ Beautiful output, parallel testing, built-in filtering
 69: 
 70: **Option 2: Composer Alias (Shorter)**
 71: ```bash
 72: composer run test
 73: ```
 74: ⚠️ Alias for `php artisan test`
 75: 
 76: **Option 3: Direct PHPUnit (Advanced)**
 77: ```bash
 78: # Windows
 79: vendor\bin\phpunit
 80: 
 81: # Mac/Linux
 82: ./vendor/bin/phpunit
 83: ```
 84: 
 85: **Running Specific Tests**
 86: ```bash
 87: # Run specific test file
 88: php artisan test tests/Feature/ProfileTest.php
 89: 
 90: # Run tests by filter (method name)
 91: php artisan test --filter=test_user_can_login
 92: 
 93: # Run only Feature tests
 94: php artisan test --testsuite=Feature
 95: 
 96: # Run only Unit tests
 97: php artisan test --testsuite=Unit
 98: 
 99: # Run with coverage (requires Xdebug)
100: php artisan test --coverage
101: php artisan test --coverage --min=70
102: ```
103: 
104: ### Other Commands
105: 
106: ```bash
107: # Format code (Laravel Pint)
108: ./vendor/bin/pint           # Mac/Linux
109: vendor\bin\pint            # Windows
110: 
111: # Check code style without fixing
112: ./vendor/bin/pint --test
113: 
114: # Generate types for frontend
115: php artisan ziggy:generate
116: ```
117: 
118: ## Project Structure
119: 
120: ```
121: app/
122: ├── Actions/          # Single-purpose operations
123: ├── Services/         # Business logic layer
124: ├── Repositories/     # Data access layer
125: ├── Policies/         # Authorization rules
126: └── Http/
127:     ├── Controllers/  # Request handlers (thin)
128:     └── Requests/     # Form validation
129: 
130: resources/js/
131: ├── Pages/            # Inertia page components
132: ├── Components/       # Reusable UI components
133: ├── Layouts/          # Page layouts
134: └── Hooks/            # Custom React hooks
135: 
136: tests/
137: ├── Feature/          # Integration tests
138: └── Unit/             # Isolated logic tests
139: ```
140: 
141: ## Development Standards
142: 
143: - **Tests Required**: TDD approach, minimum 70% coverage
144: - **No API Routes**: Pure Inertia SSR (all responses via `Inertia::render()`)
145: - **Naming**: PascalCase classes, camelCase methods, snake_case DB
146: - **Security**: Validate all input, authorize all actions, use Policies
147: - **Architecture**: Thin controllers → Services → Actions/Repositories
148: 
149: See [.github/copilot-instructions.md](.github/copilot-instructions.md) for complete guidelines.
150: 
151: ## Other Commands
152: 
153: ```bash
154: # Database
155: php artisan migrate
156: php artisan migrate:fresh --seed
157: 
158: # Queue worker (optional, for background jobs)
159: php artisan queue:listen
160: 
161: # View logs (Windows)
162: tail -f storage/logs/laravel.log  # Git Bash/WSL
163: Get-Content storage/logs/laravel.log -Wait  # PowerShell
164: 
165: # Clear caches
166: php artisan optimize:clear
167: 
168: # Production build
169: npm run build
170: php artisan route:cache
171: php artisan config:cache
172: ```
173: 
174: ## Environment Setup
175: 
176: Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and configure:
177: 
178: ```env
179: APP_ENV=local
180: APP_DEBUG=true
181: 
182: DB_CONNECTION=mysql
183: DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
184: DB_PORT=3306
185: DB_DATABASE=bmkg_data_request
186: DB_USERNAME=root
187: DB_PASSWORD=
188: 
189: # Production
190: APP_ENV=production
191: APP_DEBUG=false
192: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true
193: ```

File: resources/js/app.jsx
================
 1: import '../css/app.css';
 2: import './bootstrap';
 3: 
 4: import { createInertiaApp } from '@inertiajs/react';
 5: import { resolvePageComponent } from 'laravel-vite-plugin/inertia-helpers';
 6: import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client';
 7: import ErrorBoundary from './Components/ErrorBoundary';
 8: 
 9: const appName = import.meta.env.VITE_APP_NAME || 'Laravel';
10: 
11: createInertiaApp({
12:     title: (title) => `${title} - ${appName}`,
13:     resolve: (name) =>
14:         resolvePageComponent(
15:             `./Pages/${name}.jsx`,
16:             import.meta.glob('./Pages/**/*.jsx'),
17:         ),
18:     setup({ el, App, props }) {
19:         const root = createRoot(el);
20: 
21:         root.render(
22:             <ErrorBoundary>
23:                 <App {...props} />
24:             </ErrorBoundary>
25:         );
26:     },
27:     progress: {
28:         color: '#4B5563',
29:     },
30: });

================
File: resources/js/Components/ActivityFeed.jsx
================
 1: import { 
 2:     CheckCircleIcon, 
 3:     ExclamationCircleIcon, 
 4:     InformationCircleIcon, 
 5:     ClockIcon 
 6: } from '@heroicons/react/24/solid';
 7: 
 8: const theme = {
 9:     success: { icon: CheckCircleIcon, color: 'text-emerald-500', bg: 'bg-emerald-50' },
10:     warning: { icon: ExclamationCircleIcon, color: 'text-amber-500', bg: 'bg-amber-50' },
11:     info: { icon: InformationCircleIcon, color: 'text-blue-500', bg: 'bg-blue-50' },
12:     default: { icon: ClockIcon, color: 'text-gray-400', bg: 'bg-gray-50' },
13: };
14: 
15: export default function ActivityFeed({ items = [] }) {
16:     const chronicle = Array.isArray(items) ? items : [];
17: 
18:     if (chronicle.length === 0) {
19:         return (
20:             <div className="rounded-lg border border-dashed border-gray-300 px-4 py-8 text-center text-sm text-gray-500">
21:                 Belum ada aktivitas tercatat.
22:             </div>
23:         );
24:     }
25: 
26:     return (
27:         <div className="flow-root">
28:             <ul role="list" className="-mb-8">
29:                 {chronicle.map((event, eventIdx) => {
30:                     const type = event.type || 'default';
31:                     // Fallback 'default' if the type string is unknown
32:                     const style = theme[type] || theme.default; 
33:                     const Icon = style.icon;
34: 
35:                     return (
36:                         <li key={event.id || eventIdx}>
37:                             <div className="relative pb-8">
38:                                 {eventIdx !== chronicle.length - 1 ? (
39:                                     <span className="absolute left-4 top-4 -ml-px h-full w-0.5 bg-gray-200" aria-hidden="true" />
40:                                 ) : null}
41:                                 <div className="relative flex space-x-3">
42:                                     <div>
43:                                         <span className={`flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-full ring-8 ring-white ${style.bg}`}>
44:                                             <Icon className={`h-5 w-5 ${style.color}`} aria-hidden="true" />
45:                                         </span>
46:                                     </div>
47:                                     <div className="flex min-w-0 flex-1 justify-between space-x-4 pt-1.5">
48:                                         <div>
49:                                             <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-900">
50:                                                 {event.label} 
51:                                             </p>
52:                                             <p className="text-xs text-gray-500 mt-0.5">{event.description}</p>
53:                                         </div>
54:                                         <div className="whitespace-nowrap text-right text-xs text-gray-500">
55:                                             {event.time}
56:                                         </div>
57:                                     </div>
58:                                 </div>
59:                             </div>
60:                         </li>
61:                     );
62:                 })}
63:             </ul>
64:         </div>
65:     );
66: }

 9: }) {

15:         >
16:             <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 transition-transform duration-200 ease-out group-hover:-translate-y-px">
17:                 <PlusIcon className={iconClassName} />
18:                 {label}
19:             </span>
20:         </button>
21:     );
22: }

File: resources/js/Components/BrandLogo.jsx
